Suppose that for any $F_{W} \in \mathcal{F}_{\text{𝐺𝑁𝑁}}^{W}$, there
exists a permutation $\sigma_{W} \in S_{n}$ such that
${F_{W}\operatorname{}{(G,H)}} = {\sigma_{W}\operatorname{}{({F_{W}\operatorname{}{(\hat{G},\hat{H})}})}}$.
Then ${F\operatorname{}{(G,H)}} = {F\operatorname{}{(\hat{G},\hat{H})}}$
holds for any $F \in \mathcal{F}_{\text{𝐺𝑁𝑁}}$.]{#A3.Thmtheorem2.p1.6.6
.ltx_text .ltx_font_italic}
:::
:::

::: {#A3.5 .ltx_proof}
###### Proof of Lemma [[C.2]{.ltx_text .ltx_ref_tag}](https://ar5iv.labs.arxiv.org/html/2209.12288#A3.Thmtheorem2 "Lemma C.2. ‣ Appendix C Separation power of graph neural networks ‣ On Representing Linear Programs by Graph Neural Networks"){.ltx_ref}. {#proof-of-lemma-c.2. .ltx_title .ltx_runin .ltx_font_italic .ltx_title_proof}

::: {#A3.5.p1 .ltx_para}
Pick an arbitrary $F \in \mathcal{F}_{\text{GNN}}$. We choose
$F_{W} \in \mathcal{F}_{\text{GNN}}^{W}$ such that

  -- ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- --
     $${{{F_{W}\operatorname{}{(G^{\prime},H^{\prime})}} = \left( {F\operatorname{}{(G^{\prime},H^{\prime})}},\ldots,{F\operatorname{}{(G^{\prime},H^{\prime})}} \right)^{\top} \in {\mathbb{R}}^{n}},{{\forall{(G^{\prime},H^{\prime})}} \in {\mathcal{G}_{m,n} \times \mathcal{H}_{m}^{V} \times \mathcal{H}_{n}^{W}}}}.$$   
  -- ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- --

Note that every entry in the output of $F_{W}$ is equal to the output of
$F$. Thus, it follows from
${F_{W}\operatorname{}{(G,H)}} = {\sigma_{W}\operatorname{}{({F_{W}\operatorname{}{(\hat{G},\hat{H})}})}}$
that
${F\operatorname{}{(G,H)}} = {F\operatorname{}{(\hat{G},\hat{H})}}$. ∎
:::
:::

::: {#A3.p2 .ltx_para}
The next lemma is similar to \[[xu2019powerful]{.ltx_ref
.ltx_missing_citation .ltx_ref_self}\]\*Theorem 3 and states that the
separation power of GNNs is at least that of the WL test.
:::

::: {#A3.Thmtheorem3 .ltx_theorem .ltx_theorem_lemma}
###### [[Lemma C.3]{#A3.Thmtheorem3.1.1.1 .ltx_text .ltx_font_bold}]{.ltx_tag .ltx_tag_theorem}[.]{#A3.Thmtheorem3.2.2 .ltx_text .ltx_font_bold} {#lemma-c.3. .ltx_title .ltx_runin .ltx_title_theorem}

::: {#A3.Thmtheorem3.p1 .ltx_para}
[Let
${{(G,H)},{(\hat{G},\hat{H})}} \in {\mathcal{G}_{m,n} \times \mathcal{H}_{m}^{V} \times \mathcal{H}_{n}^{W}}$.
If ${F\operatorname{}{(G,H)}} = {F\operatorname{}{(\hat{G},\hat{H})}}$
holds for any $F \in \mathcal{F}_{\text{𝐺𝑁𝑁}}$, then
${(G,H)} \sim {(\hat{G},\hat{H})}$.]{#A3.Thmtheorem3.p1.4.4 .ltx_text
.ltx_font_italic}
:::
:::

::: {#A3.9 .ltx_proof}
###### Proof of Lemma [[C.3]{.ltx_text .ltx_ref_tag}](https://ar5iv.labs.arxiv.org/html/2209.12288#A3.Thmtheorem3 "Lemma C.3. ‣ Appendix C Separation power of graph neural networks ‣ On Representing Linear Programs by Graph Neural Networks"){.ltx_ref}. {#proof-of-lemma-c.3. .ltx_title .ltx_runin .ltx_font_italic .ltx_title_proof}

::: {#A3.6.p1 .ltx_para}
It suffices to prove that, if $(G,H)$ can be distinguished from
$(\hat{G},\hat{H})$ by the WL test, then there exists
$F \in \mathcal{F}_{\text{GNN}}$, such that
${F\operatorname{}{(G,H)}} \neq {F\operatorname{}{(\hat{G},\hat{H})}}$.
The distinguish-ability of the WL test implies that there exists
$L \in {\mathbb{N}}$ and hash functions, $\text{HASH}_{0,V}$,
$\text{HASH}_{0,W}$, $\text{HASH}_{l,V}$, $\text{HASH}_{l,W}$,
$\text{HASH}_{l,V}^{\prime}$, and $\text{HASH}_{l,W}^{\prime}$, for
$l = {1,2,\ldots,L}$, such that

  ----------------------------------------------------- -- ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- --
  [(C.3)]{.ltx_tag .ltx_tag_equation .ltx_align_left}      $${\left\{ \left\{ C_{1}^{L,V},C_{2}^{L,V},\ldots,C_{m}^{L,V} \right\} \right\} \neq \left\{ \left\{ {\hat{C}}_{1}^{L,V},{\hat{C}}_{2}^{L,V},\ldots,{\hat{C}}_{m}^{L,V} \right\} \right\}},$$   
  ----------------------------------------------------- -- ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- --

or

  ----------------------------------------------------- -- ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- --
  [(C.4)]{.ltx_tag .ltx_tag_equation .ltx_align_left}      $${\left\{ \left\{ C_{1}^{L,W},C_{2}^{L,W},\ldots,C_{n}^{L,W} \right\} \right\} \neq \left\{ \left\{ {\hat{C}}_{1}^{L,W},{\hat{C}}_{2}^{L,W},\ldots,{\hat{C}}_{n}^{L,W} \right\} \right\}},$$   
  ----------------------------------------------------- -- ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- --

We aim to construct some GNNs such that the followings hold for any
$l = {0,1,\ldots,L}$:

-   [[(i)]{.ltx_tag .ltx_tag_item}]{#A3.I2.ix1}

    ::: {#A3.I2.ix1.p1 .ltx_para}
    $h_{i}^{l,V} = h_{i^{\prime}}^{l,V}$ implies
    $C_{i}^{l,V} = C_{i^{\prime}}^{l,V}$, for
    ${1 \leq i},{i^{\prime} \leq m}$;
    :::
-   [[(ii)]{.ltx_tag .ltx_tag_item}]{#A3.I2.ix2}

    ::: {#A3.I2.ix2.p1 .ltx_para}
    ${\hat{h}}_{i}^{l,V} = {\hat{h}}_{i^{\prime}}^{l,V}$ implies
    ${\hat{C}}_{i}^{l,V} = {\hat{C}}_{i^{\prime}}^{l,V}$, for
    ${1 \leq i},{i^{\prime} \leq m}$;
    :::
-   [[(iii)]{.ltx_tag .ltx_tag_item}]{#A3.I2.ix3}

    ::: {#A3.I2.ix3.p1 .ltx_para}
    $h_{i}^{l,V} = {\hat{h}}_{i^{\prime}}^{l,V}$ implies
    $C_{i}^{l,V} = {\hat{C}}_{i^{\prime}}^{l,V}$, for
    ${1 \leq i},{i^{\prime} \leq m}$;
    :::
-   [[(iv)]{.ltx_tag .ltx_tag_item}]{#A3.I2.ix4}

    ::: {#A3.I2.ix4.p1 .ltx_para}
    $h_{j}^{l,W} = h_{j^{\prime}}^{l,W}$ implies
    $C_{j}^{l,W} = C_{j^{\prime}}^{l,W}$, for
    ${1 \leq j},{j^{\prime} \leq n}$;
    :::
-   [[(v)]{.ltx_tag .ltx_tag_item}]{#A3.I2.ix5}

    ::: {#A3.I2.ix5.p1 .ltx_para}
    ${\hat{h}}_{j}^{l,W} = {\hat{h}}_{j^{\prime}}^{l,W}$ implies
    ${\hat{C}}_{j}^{l,W} = {\hat{C}}_{j^{\prime}}^{l,W}$, for
    ${1 \leq j},{j^{\prime} \leq n}$;
    :::
-   [[(vi)]{.ltx_tag .ltx_tag_item}]{#A3.I2.ix6}

    ::: {#A3.I2.ix6.p1 .ltx_para}
    $h_{j}^{l,W} = {\hat{h}}_{j^{\prime}}^{l,W}$ implies
    $C_{j}^{l,W} = {\hat{C}}_{j^{\prime}}^{l,W}$, for
    ${1 \leq j},{j^{\prime} \leq n}$.
    :::

It is clear that the above conditions (i)-(vi) hold for $l = 0$ as long
as we choose $f_{in}^{V}$ and $f_{in}^{W}$ that are injective on the
following two sets (not multisets) respectively:

  -- --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- --
     $${{\{ h_{1}^{V},\ldots,h_{m}^{V},{\hat{h}}_{1}^{V},\ldots,{\hat{h}}_{m}^{V}\}}\operatorname{}\text{and}\operatorname{}{\{ h_{1}^{W},\ldots,h_{n}^{W},{\hat{h}}_{1}^{W},\ldots,{\hat{h}}_{n}^{W}\}}}.$$   
  -- --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- --

We then assume that (i)-(vi) hold for some $0 \leq {l - 1} < L$, and
show that these conditions are also satisfied for $l$ if we choose
$f_{l}^{V},f_{l}^{W},g_{i}^{V},g_{l}^{W}$ properly. Let us consider the
set (not multiset):

  -- ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- --
     $$\left\{ \alpha_{1},\alpha_{2},\ldots,\alpha_{s} \right\} \subset {\mathbb{R}}^{d_{l - 1}}$$   
  -- ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- --

that collects all different values in
$h_{1}^{{l - 1},W},h_{2}^{{l - 1},W},\ldots,h_{n}^{{l - 1},W},{\hat{h}}_{1}^{{l - 1},W},{\hat{h}}_{2}^{{l - 1},W},\ldots,{\hat{h}}_{n}^{{l - 1},W}$.
Let $d_{l} \geq s$ and let $e_{p}^{d_{l}} = {(0,\ldots,0,1,0,\ldots,0)}$
be the vector in ${\mathbb{R}}^{d_{l}}$ with the $p$-th entry being $1$
and all other entries being $0$, for $1 \leq p \leq s$. Choose
$f_{l}^{W}:{{\mathbb{R}}^{d_{l - 1}}\rightarrow{\mathbb{R}}^{d_{l}}}$ as
a continuous function satisfying
${f_{l}^{W}\operatorname{}{(\alpha_{p})}} = e_{p}^{d_{l}}$,
$p = {1,2,\ldots,s}$, and choose
$g_{l}^{V}:{{{\mathbb{R}}^{d_{l - 1}} \times {\mathbb{R}}^{d_{l}}}\rightarrow{\mathbb{R}}^{d_{l}}}$
that is continuous and is injective when restricted on the set (not
multiset)

  -- ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ --
     $$\begin{matrix}                                                                                                                                                                                       
     \left\{ \left( h_{i}^{{l - 1},V},{\sum\limits_{j = 1}^{n}{E_{i,j}\operatorname{}f_{l}^{W}\operatorname{}{(h_{j}^{{l - 1},W})}}} \right):{1 \leq i \leq m} \right\} \\                                  
     {{\cup \left\{ \left( {\hat{h}}_{i}^{{l - 1},V},{\sum\limits_{j = 1}^{n}{{\hat{E}}_{i,j}\operatorname{}f_{l}^{W}\operatorname{}{({\hat{h}}_{j}^{{l - 1},W})}}} \right):{1 \leq i \leq m} \right\}}.}   
     \end{matrix}$$                                                                                                                                                                                         
  -- ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ --

Noticing that

  -- ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- --
     $${{\sum\limits_{j = 1}^{n}{E_{i,j}\operatorname{}f_{l}^{W}\operatorname{}{(h_{j}^{{l - 1},W})}}} = {\sum\limits_{p = 1}^{s}{\left( {\sum\limits_{h_{j}^{{l - 1},W} = \alpha_{p}}E_{i,j}} \right)\operatorname{}e_{p}^{d_{l}}}}},$$   
  -- ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- --

and that $\{ e_{1}^{d_{l}},e_{2}^{d_{l}},\ldots,e_{s}^{d_{l}}\}$ is
linearly independent, one can conclude that
$h_{i}^{l,V} = h_{i^{\prime}}^{l,V}$ if and only if
$h_{i}^{{l - 1},V} = h_{i^{\prime}}^{{l - 1},V}$ and
${\sum_{j = 1}^{n}{E_{i,j}\operatorname{}f_{l}^{W}\operatorname{}{(h_{j}^{{l - 1},W})}}} = {\sum_{j = 1}^{n}{E_{i^{\prime},j}\operatorname{}f_{l}^{W}\operatorname{}{(h_{j}^{{l - 1},W})}}}$,
where the second condition is equivalent to

  -- ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- --
     $${{{\sum\limits_{h_{j}^{{l - 1},W} = \alpha_{p}}E_{i,j}} = {\sum\limits_{h_{j}^{{l - 1},W} = \alpha_{p}}E_{i^{\prime},j}}},{{\forall p} \in {\{ 1,2,\ldots,s\}}}}.$$   
  -- ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- --

This, as well as the condition (iv) for $l - 1$, implies that

  -- ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- --
     $${{\sum\limits_{j = 1}^{n}{E_{i,j}\operatorname{}\text{HASH}_{l,W}^{\prime}\operatorname{}\left( C_{j}^{{l - 1},W} \right)}} = {\sum\limits_{j = 1}^{n}{E_{i^{\prime},j}\operatorname{}\text{HASH}_{l,W}^{\prime}\operatorname{}\left( C_{j}^{{l - 1},W} \right)}}},$$   
  -- ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- --

and hence that $C_{i}^{l,V} = C_{i^{\prime}}^{l,V}$ by using
$h_{i}^{{l - 1},V} = h_{i}^{{l - 1},V}$ and condition (i) for $l - 1$.
Therefore, we know that (i) is satisfied for $l$, and one can show (ii)
and (iii) for $l$ using similar arguments by taking $d_{l}$ large
enough. In addition, $f_{l}^{V}$ and $g_{l}^{W}$ can also be chosen in a
similar way such that (iv)-(vi) are satisfied for $l$.
:::

::: {#A3.7.p2 .ltx_para}
Combining ([[C.3]{.ltx_text
.ltx_ref_tag}](https://ar5iv.labs.arxiv.org/html/2209.12288#A3.E3 "C.3 ‣ Proof of Lemma C.3. ‣ Appendix C Separation power of graph neural networks ‣ On Representing Linear Programs by Graph Neural Networks"){.ltx_ref}),
([[C.4]{.ltx_text
.ltx_ref_tag}](https://ar5iv.labs.arxiv.org/html/2209.12288#A3.E4 "C.4 ‣ Proof of Lemma C.3. ‣ Appendix C Separation power of graph neural networks ‣ On Representing Linear Programs by Graph Neural Networks"){.ltx_ref}),
and condition (i)-(iv) for $L$, we obtain that

  ----------------------------------------------------- -- ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- --
  [(C.5)]{.ltx_tag .ltx_tag_equation .ltx_align_left}      $${\left\{ \left\{ h_{1}^{L,V},h_{2}^{L,V},\ldots,h_{m}^{L,V} \right\} \right\} \neq \left\{ \left\{ {\hat{h}}_{1}^{L,V},{\hat{h}}_{2}^{L,V},\ldots,{\hat{h}}_{m}^{L,V} \right\} \right\}},$$   
  ----------------------------------------------------- -- ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- --

or

  -- ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- --
     $${\left\{ \left\{ h_{1}^{L,W},h_{2}^{L,W},\ldots,h_{n}^{L,W} \right\} \right\} \neq \left\{ \left\{ {\hat{h}}_{1}^{L,W},{\hat{h}}_{2}^{L,W},\ldots,{\hat{h}}_{n}^{L,W} \right\} \right\}}.$$   
  -- ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- --

Without loss of generality, we can assume that ([[C.5]{.ltx_text
.ltx_ref_tag}](https://ar5iv.labs.arxiv.org/html/2209.12288#A3.E5 "C.5 ‣ Proof of Lemma C.3. ‣ Appendix C Separation power of graph neural networks ‣ On Representing Linear Programs by Graph Neural Networks"){.ltx_ref})
holds.
:::

::: {#A3.8.p3 .ltx_para}
Consider the set (not multiset)

  -- -------------------------------------------------------------------------------- --
     $${{\{\beta_{1},\beta_{2},\ldots,\beta_{t}\}} \subset {\mathbb{R}}^{d_{L}}},$$   
  -- -------------------------------------------------------------------------------- --

that collects all different values in
$h_{1}^{L,V},h_{2}^{L,V},\ldots,h_{m}^{L,V},{\hat{h}}_{1}^{L,V},{\hat{h}}_{2}^{L,V},\ldots,{\hat{h}}_{m}^{L,V}$.
Let $k > 1$ be a positive integer that is greater than the maximal
multiplicity of an element in the multisets
$\{{\{ h_{1}^{L,V},h_{2}^{L,V},\ldots,h_{m}^{L,V}\}}\}$ and
$\{{\{{\hat{h}}_{1}^{L,V},{\hat{h}}_{2}^{L,V},\ldots,{\hat{h}}_{m}^{L,V}\}}\}$.
There exists a continuous function
$\varphi:{{\mathbb{R}}^{d_{L}}\rightarrow{\mathbb{R}}}$ such that
${\varphi\operatorname{}{(\beta_{q})}} = k^{q}$ for
$q = {1,2,\ldots,t}$, and due to ([[C.5]{.ltx_text
.ltx_ref_tag}](https://ar5iv.labs.arxiv.org/html/2209.12288#A3.E5 "C.5 ‣ Proof of Lemma C.3. ‣ Appendix C Separation power of graph neural networks ‣ On Representing Linear Programs by Graph Neural Networks"){.ltx_ref})
and the fact that the way of writing an integer as $k$-ary expression is
unique, it hence holds that

  -- ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- --
     $${{\sum\limits_{i = 1}^{m}{\varphi\operatorname{}{(h_{i}^{L,V})}}} \neq {\sum\limits_{i = 1}^{m}{\varphi\operatorname{}{({\hat{h}}_{i}^{L,V})}}}}.$$   
  -- ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- --
:::

::: {#A3.9.p4 .ltx_para}
Set the dimension of $({L + 1})$-th layer as $1$: $d_{L + 1} = 1$, and
set $f_{{L + 1},V} = 0$, $f_{{L + 1},W} = 0$,
${g_{{L + 1},V}\operatorname{}{(h,0)}} = {\varphi\operatorname{}{(h)}}$,
and $g_{{L + 1},W} = 0$. Then we have
$h_{i}^{{L + 1},V} = {\varphi\operatorname{}{(h_{i}^{L,V})}}$,
${\hat{h}}_{i}^{{L + 1},V} = {\varphi\operatorname{}{({\hat{h}}_{i}^{L,V})}}$,
and $h_{j}^{{L + 1},W} = {\hat{h}}_{j}^{{L + 1},W}$ for
$i = {1,2,\ldots,m}$ and $j = {1,2,\cdots,n}$. Define
$f_{\text{out}}:{{{\mathbb{R}} \times {\mathbb{R}}}\rightarrow{\mathbb{R}}}$
via ${f_{\text{out}}\operatorname{}{(h,h^{\prime})}} = h$. Then it
follows that

  -- ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ --
     $f_{\text{out}}\operatorname{}\left( {\sum\limits_{i = 1}^{m}h_{i}^{{L + 1},V}},{\sum\limits_{j = 1}^{n}h_{j}^{{L + 1},W}} \right)$   $= {\sum\limits_{i = 1}^{m}{\varphi\operatorname{}{(h_{i}^{L,V})}}}$                                                                                                                                                                       
                                                                                                                                           ${\neq {\sum\limits_{i = 1}^{m}{\varphi\operatorname{}{({\hat{h}}_{i}^{L,V})}}} = {f_{\text{out}}\operatorname{}\left( {\sum\limits_{i = 1}^{m}{\hat{h}}_{i}^{{L + 1},V}},{\sum\limits_{j = 1}^{n}{\hat{h}}_{j}^{{L + 1},W}} \right)}},$   
  -- ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ --

which guarantees the existence of $F \in \mathcal{F}_{\text{GNN}}$ that
has $L + 1$ layers and satisfies
${F\operatorname{}{(G,H)}} \neq {F\operatorname{}{(\hat{G},\hat{H})}}$.
∎
:::
:::

::: {#A3.10 .ltx_proof}
###### Proof of Theorem [[4.2]{.ltx_text .ltx_ref_tag}](https://ar5iv.labs.arxiv.org/html/2209.12288#S4.Thmtheorem2 "Theorem 4.2. ‣ Separation power ‣ 4. Sketch of proof ‣ On Representing Linear Programs by Graph Neural Networks"){.ltx_ref}. {#proof-of-theorem-4.2. .ltx_title .ltx_runin .ltx_font_italic .ltx_title_proof}

::: {#A3.10.p1 .ltx_para}
The equivalence of the three conditions follow directly from
Lemma [[C.1]{.ltx_text
.ltx_ref_tag}](https://ar5iv.labs.arxiv.org/html/2209.12288#A3.Thmtheorem1 "Lemma C.1. ‣ Appendix C Separation power of graph neural networks ‣ On Representing Linear Programs by Graph Neural Networks"){.ltx_ref},
[[C.2]{.ltx_text
.ltx_ref_tag}](https://ar5iv.labs.arxiv.org/html/2209.12288#A3.Thmtheorem2 "Lemma C.2. ‣ Appendix C Separation power of graph neural networks ‣ On Representing Linear Programs by Graph Neural Networks"){.ltx_ref},
and [[C.3]{.ltx_text
.ltx_ref_tag}](https://ar5iv.labs.arxiv.org/html/2209.12288#A3.Thmtheorem3 "Lemma C.3. ‣ Appendix C Separation power of graph neural networks ‣ On Representing Linear Programs by Graph Neural Networks"){.ltx_ref}.
∎
:::
:::

::: {#A3.Thmtheorem4 .ltx_theorem .ltx_theorem_corollary}
###### [[Corollary C.4]{#A3.Thmtheorem4.1.1.1 .ltx_text .ltx_font_bold}]{.ltx_tag .ltx_tag_theorem}[.]{#A3.Thmtheorem4.2.2 .ltx_text .ltx_font_bold} {#corollary-c.4. .ltx_title .ltx_runin .ltx_title_theorem}

::: {#A3.Thmtheorem4.p1 .ltx_para}
[For any two weighted bipartite graphs with vertex features
${{(G,H)},{(\hat{G},\hat{H})}} \in {\mathcal{G}_{m,n} \times \mathcal{H}_{m}^{V} \times \mathcal{H}_{n}^{W}}$,
the followings are equivalent:]{#A3.Thmtheorem4.p1.1.1 .ltx_text
.ltx_font_italic}

-   [[(i)]{.ltx_tag .ltx_tag_item}]{#A3.I3.ix1}

    ::: {#A3.I3.ix1.p1 .ltx_para}
    ${(G,H)}\overset{W}{\sim}{(\hat{G},\hat{H})}$[.]{#A3.I3.ix1.p1.1.1
    .ltx_text .ltx_font_italic}
    :::
-   [[(ii)]{.ltx_tag .ltx_tag_item}]{#A3.I3.ix2}

    ::: {#A3.I3.ix2.p1 .ltx_para}
    [For any ]{#A3.I3.ix2.p1.2.1 .ltx_text
    .ltx_font_italic}$F_{W} \in \mathcal{F}_{\text{𝐺𝑁𝑁}}^{W}$[, it holds
    that ]{#A3.I3.ix2.p1.2.2 .ltx_text
    .ltx_font_italic}${F_{W}\operatorname{}{(G,H)}} = {F_{W}\operatorname{}{(\hat{G},\hat{H})}}$[.]{#A3.I3.ix2.p1.2.3
    .ltx_text .ltx_font_italic}
    :::
:::
:::

::: {#A3.11 .ltx_proof}
###### Proof of Corollary [[C.4]{.ltx_text .ltx_ref_tag}](https://ar5iv.labs.arxiv.org/html/2209.12288#A3.Thmtheorem4 "Corollary C.4. ‣ Appendix C Separation power of graph neural networks ‣ On Representing Linear Programs by Graph Neural Networks"){.ltx_ref}. {#proof-of-corollary-c.4. .ltx_title .ltx_runin .ltx_font_italic .ltx_title_proof}

::: {#A3.11.p1 .ltx_para}
The proof follows similar lines as in the proof of
Theorem [[4.2]{.ltx_text
.ltx_ref_tag}](https://ar5iv.labs.arxiv.org/html/2209.12288#S4.Thmtheorem2 "Theorem 4.2. ‣ Separation power ‣ 4. Sketch of proof ‣ On Representing Linear Programs by Graph Neural Networks"){.ltx_ref}
with the difference that there is no permutation on
$\{ w_{1},w_{2},\ldots,w_{n}\}$. ∎
:::
:::

::: {#A3.p3 .ltx_para}
In addition to the separation power of GNNs for two weighted bipartite
graphs with vertex features, one can also obtain results on separating
different vertices in one weighted bipartite graph with vertex features.
:::

::: {#A3.Thmtheorem5 .ltx_theorem .ltx_theorem_corollary}
###### [[Corollary C.5]{#A3.Thmtheorem5.1.1.1 .ltx_text .ltx_font_bold}]{.ltx_tag .ltx_tag_theorem}[.]{#A3.Thmtheorem5.2.2 .ltx_text .ltx_font_bold} {#corollary-c.5. .ltx_title .ltx_runin .ltx_title_theorem}

::: {#A3.Thmtheorem5.p1 .ltx_para}
[For any weighted bipartite graph with vertex features $(G,H)$ and any
${j,j^{\prime}} \in {\{ 1,2,\ldots,n\}}$, the followings are
equivalent:]{#A3.Thmtheorem5.p1.2.2 .ltx_text .ltx_font_italic}

-   [[(i)]{.ltx_tag .ltx_tag_item}]{#A3.I4.ix1}

    ::: {#A3.I4.ix1.p1 .ltx_para}
    $C_{j}^{l,W} = C_{j^{\prime}}^{l,W}$[ holds for any
    ]{#A3.I4.ix1.p1.2.1 .ltx_text .ltx_font_italic}$l \in {\mathbb{N}}$[
    and any choice of hash functions.]{#A3.I4.ix1.p1.2.2 .ltx_text
    .ltx_font_italic}
    :::
-   [[(ii)]{.ltx_tag .ltx_tag_item}]{#A3.I4.ix2}

    ::: {#A3.I4.ix2.p1 .ltx_para}
    ${F_{W}\operatorname{}{(G,H)}_{j}} = {F_{W}\operatorname{}{(G,H)}_{j^{\prime}}}$[,
    ]{#A3.I4.ix2.p1.2.1 .ltx_text
    .ltx_font_italic}${\forall F_{W}} \in \mathcal{F}_{\text{𝐺𝑁𝑁}}^{W}$[.]{#A3.I4.ix2.p1.2.2
    .ltx_text .ltx_font_italic}
    :::
:::
:::

::: {#A3.12 .ltx_proof}
###### Proof of Corollary [[C.5]{.ltx_text .ltx_ref_tag}](https://ar5iv.labs.arxiv.org/html/2209.12288#A3.Thmtheorem5 "Corollary C.5. ‣ Appendix C Separation power of graph neural networks ‣ On Representing Linear Programs by Graph Neural Networks"){.ltx_ref}. {#proof-of-corollary-c.5. .ltx_title .ltx_runin .ltx_font_italic .ltx_title_proof}

::: {#A3.12.p1 .ltx_para}
"(i)$\Longrightarrow$(ii)" and "(ii)$\Longrightarrow$(i)" can be proved
using similar arguments in the proof of Lemma [[C.1]{.ltx_text
.ltx_ref_tag}](https://ar5iv.labs.arxiv.org/html/2209.12288#A3.Thmtheorem1 "Lemma C.1. ‣ Appendix C Separation power of graph neural networks ‣ On Representing Linear Programs by Graph Neural Networks"){.ltx_ref}
and Lemma [[C.3]{.ltx_text
.ltx_ref_tag}](https://ar5iv.labs.arxiv.org/html/2209.12288#A3.Thmtheorem3 "Lemma C.3. ‣ Appendix C Separation power of graph neural networks ‣ On Representing Linear Programs by Graph Neural Networks"){.ltx_ref},
respectively. ∎
:::
:::
:::

::: {#A4 .section .ltx_appendix .ltx_indent_first}
## [Appendix D ]{.ltx_tag .ltx_tag_appendix}Universal approxiation of $\mathcal{F}_{\text{GNN}}$ {#appendix-d-universal-approxiation-of-mathcalf_textgnn .ltx_title .ltx_title_appendix}

::: {#A4.p1 .ltx_para}
This section provides the proof of Theorem [[4.3]{.ltx_text
.ltx_ref_tag}](https://ar5iv.labs.arxiv.org/html/2209.12288#S4.Thmtheorem3 "Theorem 4.3. ‣ Representation power ‣ 4. Sketch of proof ‣ On Representing Linear Programs by Graph Neural Networks"){.ltx_ref}.
The main mathematical tool used in the proof is the Stone--Weierstrass
theorem:
:::

::: {#A4.Thmtheorem1 .ltx_theorem .ltx_theorem_theorem}
###### [[Theorem D.1]{#A4.Thmtheorem1.1.1.1 .ltx_text .ltx_font_bold}]{.ltx_tag .ltx_tag_theorem}[ ]{#A4.Thmtheorem1.2.2 .ltx_text .ltx_font_bold}(Stone--Weierstrass theorem \[[rudin1991functional]{.ltx_ref .ltx_missing_citation .ltx_ref_self}\]\*Section 5.7)[.]{#A4.Thmtheorem1.3.3 .ltx_text .ltx_font_bold} {#theorem-d.1-stoneweierstrass-theorem-rudin1991functionalsection-5.7. .ltx_title .ltx_runin .ltx_title_theorem}

::: {#A4.Thmtheorem1.p1 .ltx_para}
[Let $X$ be a compact Hausdorff space and let
$\mathcal{F} \subset {\mathcal{C}\operatorname{}{(X,{\mathbb{R}})}}$ be
a subalgebra. If $\mathcal{F}$ separates points on $X$, i.e., for any
${x,x^{\prime}} \in X$ with $x \neq x^{\prime}$, there exists
$F \in \mathcal{F}$ such that
${F\operatorname{}{(x)}} \neq {F\operatorname{}{(x^{\prime})}}$, and
$1 \in \mathcal{F}$, then $\mathcal{F}$ is dense in
$\mathcal{C}\operatorname{}{(X,{\mathbb{R}})}$ with the topology of
uniform convergence.]{#A4.Thmtheorem1.p1.11.11 .ltx_text
.ltx_font_italic}
:::
:::

::: {#A4.4 .ltx_proof}
###### Proof of Theorem [[4.3]{.ltx_text .ltx_ref_tag}](https://ar5iv.labs.arxiv.org/html/2209.12288#S4.Thmtheorem3 "Theorem 4.3. ‣ Representation power ‣ 4. Sketch of proof ‣ On Representing Linear Programs by Graph Neural Networks"){.ltx_ref}. {#proof-of-theorem-4.3. .ltx_title .ltx_runin .ltx_font_italic .ltx_title_proof}

::: {#A4.3.p1 .ltx_para}
Let $\pi:X\rightarrow X/ \sim$ be the quotient map, where
$\pi{(X)} = X/ \sim$ is equipped with the quotient topology. For any
$F \in \mathcal{F}_{\text{GNN}}$, since $F:{X\rightarrow{\mathbb{R}}}$
is continuous and by Theorem [[4.2]{.ltx_text
.ltx_ref_tag}](https://ar5iv.labs.arxiv.org/html/2209.12288#S4.Thmtheorem2 "Theorem 4.2. ‣ Separation power ‣ 4. Sketch of proof ‣ On Representing Linear Programs by Graph Neural Networks"){.ltx_ref},
${{F\operatorname{}{(G,H)}} = {F\operatorname{}{(\hat{G},\hat{H})}}},{{\forall{(G,H)}} \sim {(\hat{G},\hat{H})}}$,
there exists a unique continuous
$\overset{\sim}{F}:{{\pi\operatorname{}{(X)}}\rightarrow{\mathbb{R}}}$
such that $F = {\overset{\sim}{F} \circ \pi}$. Set

  -- -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- --
     $${{\overset{\sim}{\mathcal{F}}}_{\text{GNN}} = \left\{ \overset{\sim}{F}:{F \in \mathcal{F}_{\text{GNN}}} \right\} \subset {\mathcal{C}\operatorname{}{({\pi\operatorname{}{(X)}},{\mathbb{R}})}}}.$$   
  -- -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- --

In addition, the assumption on $\Phi$, i.e.,

  -- ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ --
     $${{{\Phi\operatorname{}{(G,H)}} = {\Phi\operatorname{}{(\hat{G},\hat{H})}}},{{\forall{(G,H)}} \sim {(\hat{G},\hat{H})}}},$$   
  -- ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ --

leads to the existence of a unique
$\overset{\sim}{\Phi} \in {\mathcal{C}\operatorname{}{({\pi\operatorname{}{(X)}},{\mathbb{R}})}}$
with $\Phi = {\overset{\sim}{\Phi} \circ \pi}$.
:::

::: {#A4.4.p2 .ltx_para}
Since $X$ is compact, then $\pi\operatorname{}{(X)}$ is also compact due
to the continuity of $\pi$. According to Lemma [[D.2]{.ltx_text
.ltx_ref_tag}](https://ar5iv.labs.arxiv.org/html/2209.12288#A4.Thmtheorem2 "Lemma D.2. ‣ Appendix D Universal approxiation of ℱ_"GNN" ‣ On Representing Linear Programs by Graph Neural Networks"){.ltx_ref}
below, ${\overset{\sim}{\mathcal{F}}}_{\text{GNN}}$ is a subalgebra of
$\mathcal{C}\operatorname{}{({\pi\operatorname{}{(X)}},{\mathbb{R}})}$.
By Theorem [[4.2]{.ltx_text
.ltx_ref_tag}](https://ar5iv.labs.arxiv.org/html/2209.12288#S4.Thmtheorem2 "Theorem 4.2. ‣ Separation power ‣ 4. Sketch of proof ‣ On Representing Linear Programs by Graph Neural Networks"){.ltx_ref},
${\overset{\sim}{\mathcal{F}}}_{\text{GNN}}$ separates points on
$\pi\operatorname{}{(X)}$. This can further imply that
$\pi\operatorname{}{(X)}$ is Hausdorff. In fact, for any
${x,x^{\prime}} \in {\pi\operatorname{}{(X)}}$, there exists
$\overset{\sim}{F} \in {\overset{\sim}{F}}_{\text{GNN}}$ with
${\overset{\sim}{F}\operatorname{}{(x)}} \neq {\overset{\sim}{F}\operatorname{}{(x^{\prime})}}$.
Without loss of generality, we assume that
${\overset{\sim}{F}\operatorname{}{(x)}} < {\overset{\sim}{F}\operatorname{}{(x^{\prime})}}$
and choose some $c \in {\mathbb{R}}$ with
${\overset{\sim}{F}\operatorname{}{(x)}} < c < {\overset{\sim}{F}\operatorname{}{(x^{\prime})}}$.
By continuity of $\overset{\sim}{F}$, we know that
${{\overset{\sim}{F}}^{- 1}\operatorname{}{({({- \infty},c)})}} \cap {\pi\operatorname{}{(X)}}$
and
${{\overset{\sim}{F}}^{- 1}\operatorname{}{({(c,{+ \infty})})}} \cap {\pi\operatorname{}{(X)}}$
are disjoint open subsets of $\pi\operatorname{}{(X)}$ with
$x \in {{{\overset{\sim}{F}}^{- 1}\operatorname{}{({({- \infty},c)})}} \cap {\pi\operatorname{}{(X)}}}$
and
$x^{\prime} \in {{{\overset{\sim}{F}}^{- 1}\operatorname{}{({(c,{+ \infty})})}} \cap {\pi\operatorname{}{(X)}}}$,
which leads to the Hausdorff property of $\pi\operatorname{}{(X)}$. Note
also that $1 \in {\overset{\sim}{\mathcal{F}}}_{\text{GNN}}$. Using
Theorem [[D.1]{.ltx_text
.ltx_ref_tag}](https://ar5iv.labs.arxiv.org/html/2209.12288#A4.Thmtheorem1 "Theorem D.1 (Stone–Weierstrass theorem [rudin1991functional]*Section 5.7). ‣ Appendix D Universal approxiation of ℱ_"GNN" ‣ On Representing Linear Programs by Graph Neural Networks"){.ltx_ref},
we can conclude the denseness of
${\overset{\sim}{\mathcal{F}}}_{\text{GNN}}$ in
$\mathcal{C}\operatorname{}{({\pi\operatorname{}{(X)}},{\mathbb{R}})}$.
Therefore, for any $\epsilon > 0$, there exists
$F \in \mathcal{F}_{\text{GNN}}$, such that

  -- ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- --
     $${{\sup\limits_{{(G,H)} \in X}{|{{\Phi\operatorname{}{(G,H)}} - {F\operatorname{}{(G,H)}}}|}} = {\sup\limits_{x \in {\pi\operatorname{}{(X)}}}{|{{\overset{\sim}{\Phi}\operatorname{}{(x)}} - {\overset{\sim}{F}\operatorname{}{(x)}}}|}} < \epsilon},$$   
  -- ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- --

which completes the proof. ∎
:::
:::

::: {#A4.Thmtheorem2 .ltx_theorem .ltx_theorem_lemma}
###### [[Lemma D.2]{#A4.Thmtheorem2.1.1.1 .ltx_text .ltx_font_bold}]{.ltx_tag .ltx_tag_theorem}[.]{#A4.Thmtheorem2.2.2 .ltx_text .ltx_font_bold} {#lemma-d.2. .ltx_title .ltx_runin .ltx_title_theorem}

::: {#A4.Thmtheorem2.p1 .ltx_para}
$\mathcal{F}_{\text{𝐺𝑁𝑁}}$[ is a subalgebra of
$\mathcal{C}\operatorname{}{({\mathcal{G}_{m,n} \times \mathcal{H}_{m}^{V} \times \mathcal{H}_{n}^{W}},{\mathbb{R}})}$,
and as a corollary, ${\overset{\sim}{\mathcal{F}}}_{\text{𝐺𝑁𝑁}}$ is a
subalgebra of
$\mathcal{C}{(\mathcal{G}_{m,n} \times \mathcal{H}_{m}^{V} \times \mathcal{H}_{n}^{W}/ \sim ,{\mathbb{R}})}$.]{#A4.Thmtheorem2.p1.4.3
.ltx_text .ltx_font_italic}
:::
:::

::: {#A4.5 .ltx_proof}
###### Proof of Lemma [[D.2]{.ltx_text .ltx_ref_tag}](https://ar5iv.labs.arxiv.org/html/2209.12288#A4.Thmtheorem2 "Lemma D.2. ‣ Appendix D Universal approxiation of ℱ_"GNN" ‣ On Representing Linear Programs by Graph Neural Networks"){.ltx_ref}. {#proof-of-lemma-d.2. .ltx_title .ltx_runin .ltx_font_italic .ltx_title_proof}

::: {#A4.5.p1 .ltx_para}
It suffices to show that $\mathcal{F}_{\text{GNN}}$ is closed under
addition and multiplication. Consider any
${F,\hat{F}} \in \mathcal{F}_{\text{GNN}}$. Thanks to
Lemma [[D.3]{.ltx_text
.ltx_ref_tag}](https://ar5iv.labs.arxiv.org/html/2209.12288#A4.Thmtheorem3 "Lemma D.3. ‣ The output layer ‣ Appendix D Universal approxiation of ℱ_"GNN" ‣ On Representing Linear Programs by Graph Neural Networks"){.ltx_ref},
we can assume that both $F$ and $\hat{F}$ have $L$ layers. Suppose that
$F$ is constructed by

  -- -- ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ --
        ${f_{in}^{V}:{\mathcal{H}^{V}\rightarrow{{\mathbb{R}}^{d_{0}},f_{in}^{W}}}:{\mathcal{H}^{W}\rightarrow{\mathbb{R}}^{d_{0}}}},$                                                                                           
        ${{f_{l}^{V},f_{l}^{W}}:{{\mathbb{R}}^{d_{l - 1}}\rightarrow{{\mathbb{R}}^{d_{l}},g_{l}^{V},g_{l}^{W}}}:{{{{\mathbb{R}}^{d_{l - 1}} \times {\mathbb{R}}^{d_{l}}}\rightarrow{\mathbb{R}}^{d_{l}}},{1 \leq l \leq L}}},$   
        ${f_{\text{out}}:{{{\mathbb{R}}^{d_{L}} \times {\mathbb{R}}^{d_{L}}}\rightarrow{\mathbb{R}}}},$                                                                                                                          
  -- -- ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ --

and that $\hat{F}$ is constructed by

  -- -- ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ --
        ${{\hat{f}}_{in}^{V}:{\mathcal{H}^{V}\rightarrow{{\mathbb{R}}^{{\hat{d}}_{0}},{\hat{f}}_{in}^{W}}}:{\mathcal{H}^{W}\rightarrow{\mathbb{R}}^{{\hat{d}}_{0}}}},$                                                                                                                                   
        ${{{\hat{f}}_{l}^{V},{\hat{f}}_{l}^{W}}:{{\mathbb{R}}^{{\hat{d}}_{l - 1}}\rightarrow{{\mathbb{R}}^{{\hat{d}}_{l}},{\hat{g}}_{l}^{V},{\hat{g}}_{l}^{W}}}:{{{{\mathbb{R}}^{{\hat{d}}_{l - 1}} \times {\mathbb{R}}^{{\hat{d}}_{l}}}\rightarrow{\mathbb{R}}^{{\hat{d}}_{l}}},{1 \leq l \leq L}}},$   
        ${{\hat{f}}_{\text{out}}:{{{\mathbb{R}}^{{\hat{d}}_{L}} \times {\mathbb{R}}^{{\hat{d}}_{L}}}\rightarrow{\mathbb{R}}}}.$                                                                                                                                                                          
  -- -- ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ --

One can then construct two new GNNs computing $F + \hat{F}$ and
$F \cdot \hat{F}$ as follows:
:::
:::

::: {#A4.SS0.SSS0.Px1 .section .ltx_paragraph .ltx_indentfirst}
#### The input layer {#the-input-layer .ltx_title .ltx_font_bold .ltx_title_paragraph}

::: {#A4.SS0.SSS0.Px1.p1 .ltx_para}
The update rule of the input layer is defined by

  -- ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- --
     $$\begin{aligned}                                                                                                            
     {\mathbf{f}_{in}^{V}:\mathcal{H}^{V}} & {{\rightarrow\quad{{\mathbb{R}}^{d_{0}} \times {\mathbb{R}}^{{\hat{d}}_{0}}}},} \\   
     {h\quad} & {{\mapsto\left( {f_{in}^{V}\operatorname{}{(h)}},{{\hat{f}}_{in}^{V}\operatorname{}{(h)}} \right)},}              
     \end{aligned}$$                                                                                                              
  -- ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- --

  -- ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- --
     $$\begin{aligned}                                                                                                            
     {\mathbf{f}_{in}^{W}:\mathcal{H}^{W}} & {{\rightarrow\quad{{\mathbb{R}}^{d_{0}} \times {\mathbb{R}}^{{\hat{d}}_{0}}}},} \\   
     {h\quad} & {{\mapsto\left( {f_{in}^{W}\operatorname{}{(h)}},{{\hat{f}}_{in}^{W}\operatorname{}{(h)}} \right)}.}              
     \end{aligned}$$                                                                                                              
  -- ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- --

Then the vertex features after the computation of the input layer
$\mathbf{h}_{i}^{0,V}$ and $\mathbf{h}_{j}^{0,W}$ are given by:

  -- ------------------------ -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- --
     $\mathbf{h}_{i}^{0,V}$   ${= {\mathbf{f}_{in}^{V}\operatorname{}{(h_{i}^{V})}} = \left( {f_{in}^{V}\operatorname{}{(h_{i}^{V})}},{{\hat{f}}_{in}^{V}\operatorname{}{(h_{i}^{V})}} \right) = {(h_{i}^{0,V},{\hat{h}}_{i}^{0,V})} \in {{\mathbb{R}}^{d_{0}} \times {\mathbb{R}}^{{\hat{d}}_{0}}}},$   
     $\mathbf{h}_{j}^{0,W}$   ${= {\mathbf{f}_{in}^{W}\operatorname{}{(h_{j}^{W})}} = \left( {f_{in}^{W}\operatorname{}{(h_{j}^{W})}},{{\hat{f}}_{in}^{W}\operatorname{}{(h_{j}^{W})}} \right) = {(h_{j}^{0,W},{\hat{h}}_{j}^{0,W})} \in {{\mathbb{R}}^{d_{0}} \times {\mathbb{R}}^{{\hat{d}}_{0}}}},$   
  -- ------------------------ -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- --

for $i = {1,{2\operatorname{}\ldots},m}$, and $j = {1,2,\ldots,n}$.
:::
:::

::: {#A4.SS0.SSS0.Px2 .section .ltx_paragraph .ltx_indentfirst}
#### The $l$-th layer ($1 \leq l \leq L$) {#the-l-th-layer-1-leq-l-leq-l .ltx_title .ltx_font_bold .ltx_title_paragraph}

::: {#A4.SS0.SSS0.Px2.p1 .ltx_para}
We set

  -- ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ --
     $$\begin{aligned}                                                                                                                                                              
     {\mathbf{f}_{l}^{V}:{{\mathbb{R}}^{d_{l - 1}} \times {\mathbb{R}}^{{\hat{d}}_{l - 1}}}} & {{\rightarrow\quad{{\mathbb{R}}^{d_{l}} \times {\mathbb{R}}^{{\hat{d}}_{l}}}},} \\   
     {(h,\hat{h})} & {{\mapsto\left( {f_{l}^{V}\operatorname{}{(h)}},{{\hat{f}}_{l}^{V}\operatorname{}{(\hat{h})}} \right)},}                                                       
     \end{aligned}$$                                                                                                                                                                
  -- ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ --

  -- ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ --
     $$\begin{aligned}                                                                                                                                                              
     {\mathbf{f}_{l}^{W}:{{\mathbb{R}}^{d_{l - 1}} \times {\mathbb{R}}^{{\hat{d}}_{l - 1}}}} & {{\rightarrow\quad{{\mathbb{R}}^{d_{l}} \times {\mathbb{R}}^{{\hat{d}}_{l}}}},} \\   
     {(h,\hat{h})} & {{\mapsto\left( {f_{l}^{W}\operatorname{}{(h)}},{{\hat{f}}_{l}^{W}\operatorname{}{(\hat{h})}} \right)},}                                                       
     \end{aligned}$$                                                                                                                                                                
  -- ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ --

  -- ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- --
     $$\begin{aligned}                                                                                                                                                                                                                              
     {\mathbf{g}_{l}^{V}:{{\mathbb{R}}^{d_{l - 1}} \times {\mathbb{R}}^{{\hat{d}}_{l - 1}} \times {\mathbb{R}}^{d_{l}} \times {\mathbb{R}}^{{\hat{d}}_{l}}}} & {{\rightarrow\quad{{\mathbb{R}}^{d_{l}} \times {\mathbb{R}}^{{\hat{d}}_{l}}}},} \\   
     {{(h,\hat{h},h^{\prime},{\hat{h}}^{\prime})}\quad} & {{\mapsto\left( {g_{l}^{W}\operatorname{}{(h,h^{\prime})}},{{\hat{g}}_{l}^{W}\operatorname{}{(\hat{h},{\hat{h}}^{\prime})}} \right)},}                                                    
     \end{aligned}$$                                                                                                                                                                                                                                
  -- ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- --

and

  -- ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- --
     $$\begin{aligned}                                                                                                                                                                                                                              
     {\mathbf{g}_{l}^{W}:{{\mathbb{R}}^{d_{l - 1}} \times {\mathbb{R}}^{{\hat{d}}_{l - 1}} \times {\mathbb{R}}^{d_{l}} \times {\mathbb{R}}^{{\hat{d}}_{l}}}} & {{\rightarrow\quad{{\mathbb{R}}^{d_{l}} \times {\mathbb{R}}^{{\hat{d}}_{l}}}},} \\   
     {{(h,\hat{h},h^{\prime},{\hat{h}}^{\prime})}\quad} & {{\mapsto\left( {g_{l}^{V}\operatorname{}{(h,h^{\prime})}},{{\hat{g}}_{l}^{V}\operatorname{}{(\hat{h},{\hat{h}}^{\prime})}} \right)}.}                                                    
     \end{aligned}$$                                                                                                                                                                                                                                
  -- ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- --

Then the vertex features after the computation of the $l$-th layer
$\mathbf{h}_{i}^{l,V}$ and $\mathbf{h}_{j}^{l,W}$ are given by:

  -- ------------------------ ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ --
     $\mathbf{h}_{i}^{l,V}$   $= {\mathbf{g}_{l}^{V}\operatorname{}\left( \mathbf{h}_{i}^{{l - 1},V},{\sum\limits_{j = 1}^{n}{E_{i,j}\operatorname{}\mathbf{f}_{l}^{W}\operatorname{}{(\mathbf{h}_{j}^{{l - 1},W})}}} \right)}$                                                                                                                                                                        
                              $= \left( {g_{l}^{V}\operatorname{}\left( h_{i}^{{l - 1},V},{\sum\limits_{j = 1}^{n}{E_{i,j}\operatorname{}f_{l}^{W}\operatorname{}{(h_{j}^{{l - 1},W})}}} \right)},{{\hat{g}}_{l}^{V}\operatorname{}\left( {\hat{h}}_{i}^{{l - 1},V},{\sum\limits_{j = 1}^{n}{E_{i,j}\operatorname{}{\hat{f}}_{l}^{W}\operatorname{}{({\hat{h}}_{j}^{{l - 1},W})}}} \right)} \right)$   
                              ${= {(h_{i}^{l,V},{\hat{h}}_{i}^{l,V})} \in {{\mathbb{R}}^{d_{l}} \times {\mathbb{R}}^{{\hat{d}}_{l}}}},$                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                
  -- ------------------------ ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ --

and

  -- ------------------------ ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ --
     $\mathbf{h}_{j}^{l,W}$   $= {\mathbf{g}_{l}^{W}\operatorname{}\left( \mathbf{h}_{j}^{{l - 1},W},{\sum\limits_{i = 1}^{m}{E_{i,j}\operatorname{}\mathbf{f}_{l}^{V}\operatorname{}{(\mathbf{h}_{i}^{{l - 1},V})}}} \right)}$                                                                                                                                                                        
                              $= \left( {g_{l}^{W}\operatorname{}\left( h_{j}^{{l - 1},W},{\sum\limits_{i = 1}^{m}{E_{i,j}\operatorname{}f_{l}^{V}\operatorname{}{(h_{i}^{{l - 1},V})}}} \right)},{{\hat{g}}_{l}^{W}\operatorname{}\left( {\hat{h}}_{j}^{{l - 1},W},{\sum\limits_{i = 1}^{m}{E_{i,j}\operatorname{}{\hat{f}}_{l}^{V}\operatorname{}{({\hat{h}}_{i}^{{l - 1},V})}}} \right)} \right)$   
                              ${= {(h_{j}^{l,W},{\hat{h}}_{j}^{l,W})} \in {{\mathbb{R}}^{d_{l}} \times {\mathbb{R}}^{{\hat{d}}_{l}}}},$                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                
  -- ------------------------ ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ --

for $i = {1,{2\operatorname{}\ldots},m}$, and $j = {1,2,\ldots,n}$.
:::
:::

::: {#A4.SS0.SSS0.Px3 .section .ltx_paragraph .ltx_indentfirst}
#### The output layer {#the-output-layer .ltx_title .ltx_font_bold .ltx_title_paragraph}

::: {#A4.SS0.SSS0.Px3.p1 .ltx_para}
To obtain $F + \hat{F}$, we set

  -- ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- --
     $$\begin{aligned}                                                                                                                                                                                                
     {\text{𝐟}_{\text{out}}^{\text{add}}:{{\mathbb{R}}^{d_{L}} \times {\mathbb{R}}^{{\hat{d}}_{L}} \times {\mathbb{R}}^{d_{L}} \times {\mathbb{R}}^{{\hat{d}}_{L}}}} & {{\rightarrow\mspace{90mu}{\mathbb{R}}},} \\   
     {({(h,\hat{h})},{(h^{\prime},{\hat{h}}^{\prime})})} & {{\mapsto{{f_{\text{out}}\operatorname{}{(h,h^{\prime})}} + {{\hat{f}}_{\text{out}}\operatorname{}{(\hat{h},{\hat{h}}^{\prime})}}}}.}                      
     \end{aligned}$$                                                                                                                                                                                                  
  -- ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- --

Then it holds that

  -- --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- --
     ${\text{𝐟}_{\text{out}}^{\text{add}}\operatorname{}\left( {\sum\limits_{i = 1}^{m}\text{𝐡}_{i}^{L,V}},{\sum\limits_{j = 1}^{n}\text{𝐡}_{1}^{L,W}} \right)} =$   ${f_{\text{out}}\operatorname{}\left( {\sum\limits_{i = 1}^{m}h_{i}^{L,V}},{\sum\limits_{j = 1}^{n}h_{j}^{L,W}} \right)} + {{\hat{f}}_{\text{out}}\operatorname{}\left( {\sum\limits_{i = 1}^{m}{\hat{h}}_{i}^{L,V}},{\sum\limits_{j = 1}^{n}{\hat{h}}_{j}^{L,W}} \right)}$   
     $=$                                                                                                                                                             ${{F\operatorname{}{(G,H)}} + {\hat{F}\operatorname{}{(G,H)}}}.$                                                                                                                                                                                                              
  -- --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- --

To obtain $F \cdot \hat{F}$, we set

  -- --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- --
     $$\begin{aligned}                                                                                                                                                                                                     
     {\text{𝐟}_{\text{out}}^{\text{multiply}}:{{\mathbb{R}}^{d_{L}} \times {\mathbb{R}}^{{\hat{d}}_{L}} \times {\mathbb{R}}^{d_{L}} \times {\mathbb{R}}^{{\hat{d}}_{L}}}} & {{\rightarrow\mspace{90mu}{\mathbb{R}}},} \\   
     {({(h,\hat{h})},{(h^{\prime},{\hat{h}}^{\prime})})} & {{\mapsto{{{f_{\text{out}}\operatorname{}{(h,h^{\prime})}} \cdot {\hat{f}}_{\text{out}}}\operatorname{}{(\hat{h},{\hat{h}}^{\prime})}}}.}                       
     \end{aligned}$$                                                                                                                                                                                                       
  -- --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- --

Then it holds that

  -- -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- --
     ${\text{𝐟}_{\text{out}}^{\text{multiply}}\operatorname{}\left( {\sum\limits_{i = 1}^{m}\text{𝐡}_{i}^{L,V}},{\sum\limits_{j = 1}^{n}\text{𝐡}_{1}^{L,W}} \right)} =$   ${{f_{\text{out}}\operatorname{}\left( {\sum\limits_{i = 1}^{m}h_{i}^{L,V}},{\sum\limits_{j = 1}^{n}h_{j}^{L,W}} \right)} \cdot {\hat{f}}_{\text{out}}}\operatorname{}\left( {\sum\limits_{i = 1}^{m}{\hat{h}}_{i}^{L,V}},{\sum\limits_{j = 1}^{n}{\hat{h}}_{j}^{L,W}} \right)$   
     $=$                                                                                                                                                                  ${{{F\operatorname{}{(G,H)}} \cdot \hat{F}}\operatorname{}{(G,H)}}.$                                                                                                                                                                                                              
  -- -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- --

The constructed GNNs satisfy
${{F\operatorname{}{(G,H)}} + {\hat{F}\operatorname{}{(G,H)}}} \in \mathcal{F}_{\text{GNN}}$
and
${{{F\operatorname{}{(G,H)}} \cdot \hat{F}}\operatorname{}{(G,H)}} \in \mathcal{F}_{\text{GNN}}$,
which finishes the proof. ∎
:::

::: {#A4.Thmtheorem3 .ltx_theorem .ltx_theorem_lemma}
###### [[Lemma D.3]{#A4.Thmtheorem3.1.1.1 .ltx_text .ltx_font_bold}]{.ltx_tag .ltx_tag_theorem}[.]{#A4.Thmtheorem3.2.2 .ltx_text .ltx_font_bold} {#lemma-d.3. .ltx_title .ltx_runin .ltx_title_theorem}

::: {#A4.Thmtheorem3.p1 .ltx_para}
[If $F \in \mathcal{F}_{\text{𝐺𝑁𝑁}}$ has $L$ layers, then there exists
$\hat{F} \in \mathcal{F}_{\text{𝐺𝑁𝑁}}$ with $L + 1$ layers such that
$F = \hat{F}$. ]{#A4.Thmtheorem3.p1.5.5 .ltx_text .ltx_font_italic}
:::
:::

::: {#A4.SS0.SSS0.Px3.1 .ltx_proof}
###### Proof of Lemma [[D.3]{.ltx_text .ltx_ref_tag}](https://ar5iv.labs.arxiv.org/html/2209.12288#A4.Thmtheorem3 "Lemma D.3. ‣ The output layer ‣ Appendix D Universal approxiation of ℱ_"GNN" ‣ On Representing Linear Programs by Graph Neural Networks"){.ltx_ref}. {#proof-of-lemma-d.3. .ltx_title .ltx_runin .ltx_font_italic .ltx_title_proof}

::: {#A4.SS0.SSS0.Px3.1.p1 .ltx_para}
Suppose that $F$ is constructed by
$f_{in}^{V},f_{in}^{W},f_{\text{out}},{\{ f_{l}^{V},f_{l}^{W},g_{l}^{V},g_{l}^{W}\}}_{l = 0}^{L}$.
We choose $f_{L + 1}^{V} \equiv 0$, $f_{L + 1}^{W} \equiv 0$,
${g_{L + 1}^{V}\operatorname{}{(h,h^{\prime})}} = h$,
${g_{L + 1}^{W}\operatorname{}{(h,h^{\prime})}} = h$. Let $\hat{F}$ be
constructed by
$f_{in}^{V},f_{in}^{W},f_{\text{out}},{\{ f_{l}^{V},f_{l}^{W},g_{l}^{V},g_{l}^{W}\}}_{l = 0}^{L + 1}$.
Then $\hat{F}$ has $L + 1$ layers with $\hat{F} = F$. ∎
:::
:::
:::
:::

::: {#A5 .section .ltx_appendix .ltx_indent_first}
## [Appendix E ]{.ltx_tag .ltx_tag_appendix}Universal approximation of $\mathcal{F}_{\text{GNN}}^{W}$ {#appendix-e-universal-approximation-of-mathcalf_textgnnw .ltx_title .ltx_title_appendix}

::: {#A5.p1 .ltx_para}
This section provides an universal approximation result of
$\mathcal{F}_{\text{GNN}}^{W}$.
:::

::: {#A5.Thmtheorem1 .ltx_theorem .ltx_theorem_theorem}
###### [[Theorem E.1]{#A5.Thmtheorem1.1.1.1 .ltx_text .ltx_font_bold}]{.ltx_tag .ltx_tag_theorem}[.]{#A5.Thmtheorem1.2.2 .ltx_text .ltx_font_bold} {#theorem-e.1. .ltx_title .ltx_runin .ltx_title_theorem}

::: {#A5.Thmtheorem1.p1 .ltx_para}
[Let
$X \subset {\mathcal{G}_{m,n} \times \mathcal{H}_{m}^{V} \times \mathcal{H}_{n}^{W}}$
be a compact subset that is closed under the action of
$S_{m} \times S_{n}$. Suppose that
$\Phi \in {\mathcal{C}\operatorname{}{(X,{\mathbb{R}}^{n})}}$ satisfies
the followings:]{#A5.Thmtheorem1.p1.3.3 .ltx_text .ltx_font_italic}

-   [[(i)]{.ltx_tag .ltx_tag_item}]{#A5.I1.ix1}

    ::: {#A5.I1.ix1.p1 .ltx_para}
    [For any ]{#A5.I1.ix1.p1.3.1 .ltx_text
    .ltx_font_italic}$\sigma_{V} \in S_{m}$[, ]{#A5.I1.ix1.p1.3.2
    .ltx_text .ltx_font_italic}$\sigma_{W} \in S_{n}$[, and
    ]{#A5.I1.ix1.p1.3.3 .ltx_text .ltx_font_italic}${(G,H)} \in X$[, it
    holds that]{#A5.I1.ix1.p1.3.4 .ltx_text .ltx_font_italic}

      ----------------------------------------------------- -- --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- --
      [(E.1)]{.ltx_tag .ltx_tag_equation .ltx_align_left}      $${{\Phi\operatorname{}\left( {{(\sigma_{V},\sigma_{W})} \ast {(G,H)}} \right)} = {\sigma_{W}\operatorname{}{({\Phi\operatorname{}{(G,H)}})}}}.$$   
      ----------------------------------------------------- -- --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- --
    :::
-   [[(ii)]{.ltx_tag .ltx_tag_item}]{#A5.I1.ix2}

    ::: {#A5.I1.ix2.p1 .ltx_para}
    ${\Phi\operatorname{}{(G,H)}} = {\Phi\operatorname{}{(\hat{G},\hat{H})}}$[
    holds for all ]{#A5.I1.ix2.p1.3.1 .ltx_text
    .ltx_font_italic}${{(G,H)},{(\hat{G},\hat{H})}} \in X$[ with
    ]{#A5.I1.ix2.p1.3.2 .ltx_text
    .ltx_font_italic}${(G,H)}\overset{W}{\sim}{(\hat{G},\hat{H})}$[.]{#A5.I1.ix2.p1.3.3
    .ltx_text .ltx_font_italic}
    :::
-   [[(iii)]{.ltx_tag .ltx_tag_item}]{#A5.I1.ix3}

    ::: {#A5.I1.ix3.p1 .ltx_para}
    [Given any ]{#A5.I1.ix3.p1.6.1 .ltx_text
    .ltx_font_italic}${(G,H)} \in X$[ and any ]{#A5.I1.ix3.p1.6.2
    .ltx_text
    .ltx_font_italic}${j,j^{\prime}} \in {\{ 1,2,\ldots,n\}}$[, if
    ]{#A5.I1.ix3.p1.6.3 .ltx_text
    .ltx_font_italic}$C_{j}^{l,W} = C_{j^{\prime}}^{l,W}$[ (the vertex
    colors obtained in the ]{#A5.I1.ix3.p1.6.4 .ltx_text
    .ltx_font_italic}$l$[-th iteration in WL test) holds for any
    ]{#A5.I1.ix3.p1.6.5 .ltx_text .ltx_font_italic}$l \in {\mathbb{N}}$[
    and any choices of hash functions, then ]{#A5.I1.ix3.p1.6.6
    .ltx_text
    .ltx_font_italic}${\Phi\operatorname{}{(G,H)}_{j}} = {\Phi\operatorname{}{(G,H)}_{j^{\prime}}}$[.]{#A5.I1.ix3.p1.6.7
    .ltx_text .ltx_font_italic}
    :::

[Then for any $\epsilon > 0$, there exists
$F \in \mathcal{F}_{\text{𝐺𝑁𝑁}}^{W}$ such that]{#A5.Thmtheorem1.p1.5.2
.ltx_text .ltx_font_italic}

  -- ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- --
     $${{\sup\limits_{{(G,H)} \in X}{\|{{\Phi\operatorname{}{(G,H)}} - {F\operatorname{}{(G,H)}}}\|}} < \epsilon}.$$   
  -- ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- --
:::
:::

::: {#A5.p2 .ltx_para}
Theorem [[E.1]{.ltx_text
.ltx_ref_tag}](https://ar5iv.labs.arxiv.org/html/2209.12288#A5.Thmtheorem1 "Theorem E.1. ‣ Appendix E Universal approximation of ℱ_"GNN"^𝑊 ‣ On Representing Linear Programs by Graph Neural Networks"){.ltx_ref}
is a LP-graph version of results on the closure of equivariant GNN class
in \[[azizian2020expressive]{.ltx_ref .ltx_missing_citation
.ltx_ref_self}, [geerts2022expressiveness]{.ltx_ref
.ltx_missing_citation .ltx_ref_self}\]. The main tool in the proof of
Theorem [[E.1]{.ltx_text
.ltx_ref_tag}](https://ar5iv.labs.arxiv.org/html/2209.12288#A5.Thmtheorem1 "Theorem E.1. ‣ Appendix E Universal approximation of ℱ_"GNN"^𝑊 ‣ On Representing Linear Programs by Graph Neural Networks"){.ltx_ref}
is the following generalized Stone-Weierstrass theorem for equivariant
functions established in \[[azizian2020expressive]{.ltx_ref
.ltx_missing_citation .ltx_ref_self}\].
:::

::: {#A5.Thmtheorem2 .ltx_theorem .ltx_theorem_theorem}
###### [[Theorem E.2]{#A5.Thmtheorem2.1.1.1 .ltx_text .ltx_font_bold}]{.ltx_tag .ltx_tag_theorem}[ ]{#A5.Thmtheorem2.2.2 .ltx_text .ltx_font_bold}(Generalized Stone-Weierstrass theorem \[[azizian2020expressive]{.ltx_ref .ltx_missing_citation .ltx_ref_self}\]\*Theorem 22)[.]{#A5.Thmtheorem2.3.3 .ltx_text .ltx_font_bold} {#theorem-e.2-generalized-stone-weierstrass-theorem-azizian2020expressivetheorem-22. .ltx_title .ltx_runin .ltx_title_theorem}

::: {#A5.Thmtheorem2.p1 .ltx_para}
[Let $X$ be a compact topology space and let $\mathbf{G}$ be a finite
group that acts continuously on $X$ and ${\mathbb{R}}^{n}$. Define the
collection of all equivariant continuous functions from $X$ to
${\mathbb{R}}^{n}$ as follows:]{#A5.Thmtheorem2.p1.6.6 .ltx_text
.ltx_font_italic}

  -- --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- --
     $${{\mathcal{C}_{E}\operatorname{}{(X,{\mathbb{R}}^{n})}} = {\{{F \in {\mathcal{C}\operatorname{}{(X,{\mathbb{R}}^{n})}}}:{{{F\operatorname{}{({g \ast x})}} = {{g \ast F}\operatorname{}{(x)}}},{{{\forall x} \in X},{g \in \mathbf{G}}}}\}}}.$$   
  -- --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- --

[Consider any
$\mathcal{F} \subset {\mathcal{C}_{E}\operatorname{}{(X,{\mathbb{R}}^{n})}}$
and any
$\Phi \in {\mathcal{C}_{E}\operatorname{}{(X,{\mathbb{R}}^{n})}}$.
Suppose the following conditions hold:]{#A5.Thmtheorem2.p1.8.2 .ltx_text
.ltx_font_italic}

-   [[(i)]{.ltx_tag .ltx_tag_item}]{#A5.I2.ix1}

    ::: {#A5.I2.ix1.p1 .ltx_para}
    $\mathcal{F}$[ is a subalgebra of ]{#A5.I2.ix1.p1.3.1 .ltx_text
    .ltx_font_italic}$\mathcal{C}\operatorname{}{(X,{\mathbb{R}}^{n})}$[
    and ]{#A5.I2.ix1.p1.3.2 .ltx_text
    .ltx_font_italic}$\mathbf{1} \in \mathcal{F}$[.]{#A5.I2.ix1.p1.3.3
    .ltx_text .ltx_font_italic}
    :::
-   [[(ii)]{.ltx_tag .ltx_tag_item}]{#A5.I2.ix2}

    ::: {#A5.I2.ix2.p1 .ltx_para}
    [For any ]{#A5.I2.ix2.p1.7.1 .ltx_text
    .ltx_font_italic}${x,x^{\prime}} \in X$[, if ]{#A5.I2.ix2.p1.7.2
    .ltx_text
    .ltx_font_italic}${f\operatorname{}{(x)}} = {f\operatorname{}{(x^{\prime})}}$[
    holds for any ]{#A5.I2.ix2.p1.7.3 .ltx_text
    .ltx_font_italic}$f \in {\mathcal{C}\operatorname{}{(X,{\mathbb{R}})}}$[
    with ]{#A5.I2.ix2.p1.7.4 .ltx_text
    .ltx_font_italic}${f\operatorname{}\mathbf{1}} \in \mathcal{F}$[,
    then for any ]{#A5.I2.ix2.p1.7.5 .ltx_text
    .ltx_font_italic}$F \in \mathcal{F}$[, there exists
    ]{#A5.I2.ix2.p1.7.6 .ltx_text .ltx_font_italic}$g \in \mathbf{G}$[
    such that ]{#A5.I2.ix2.p1.7.7 .ltx_text
    .ltx_font_italic}${F\operatorname{}{(x)}} = {{g \ast F}\operatorname{}{(x^{\prime})}}$[.]{#A5.I2.ix2.p1.7.8
    .ltx_text .ltx_font_italic}
    :::
-   [[(iii)]{.ltx_tag .ltx_tag_item}]{#A5.I2.ix3}

    ::: {#A5.I2.ix3.p1 .ltx_para}
    [For any ]{#A5.I2.ix3.p1.4.1 .ltx_text
    .ltx_font_italic}${x,x^{\prime}} \in X$[, if ]{#A5.I2.ix3.p1.4.2
    .ltx_text
    .ltx_font_italic}${F\operatorname{}{(x)}} = {F\operatorname{}{(x^{\prime})}}$[
    holds for any ]{#A5.I2.ix3.p1.4.3 .ltx_text
    .ltx_font_italic}$F \in \mathcal{F}$[, then ]{#A5.I2.ix3.p1.4.4
    .ltx_text
    .ltx_font_italic}${\Phi\operatorname{}{(x)}} = {\Phi\operatorname{}{(x^{\prime})}}$[.]{#A5.I2.ix3.p1.4.5
    .ltx_text .ltx_font_italic}
    :::
-   [[(iv)]{.ltx_tag .ltx_tag_item}]{#A5.I2.ix4}

    ::: {#A5.I2.ix4.p1 .ltx_para}
    [For any ]{#A5.I2.ix4.p1.2.1 .ltx_text .ltx_font_italic}$x \in X$[,
    it holds that ]{#A5.I2.ix4.p1.2.2 .ltx_text
    .ltx_font_italic}${{\Phi\operatorname{}{(x)}_{j}} = {\Phi\operatorname{}{(x)}_{j^{\prime}}}},{{\forall{(j,j^{\prime})}} \in {J\operatorname{}{(x)}}}$[,
    where]{#A5.I2.ix4.p1.2.3 .ltx_text .ltx_font_italic}

      -- -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- --
         $${{J\operatorname{}{(x)}} = \left\{ {\{ 1,2,\ldots,n\}}^{n}:{{{F\operatorname{}{(x)}_{j}} = {F\operatorname{}{(x)}_{j^{\prime}}}},{{\forall F} \in \mathcal{F}}} \right\}}.$$   
      -- -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- --
    :::

[Then for any $\epsilon > 0$, there exists $F \in \mathcal{F}$ such
that]{#A5.Thmtheorem2.p1.10.2 .ltx_text .ltx_font_italic}

  -- ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- --
     $${{\sup\limits_{x \in X}{\|{{\Phi\operatorname{}{(x)}} - {F\operatorname{}{(x)}}}\|}} < \epsilon}.$$   
  -- ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- --
:::
:::

::: {#A5.p3 .ltx_para}
We refer to \[[timofte2005stone]{.ltx_ref .ltx_missing_citation
.ltx_ref_self}\] for different versions of Stone-Weierstrass theorem,
that is also used in \[[azizian2020expressive]{.ltx_ref
.ltx_missing_citation .ltx_ref_self}\]. In the proof of
Theorem [[E.1]{.ltx_text
.ltx_ref_tag}](https://ar5iv.labs.arxiv.org/html/2209.12288#A5.Thmtheorem1 "Theorem E.1. ‣ Appendix E Universal approximation of ℱ_"GNN"^𝑊 ‣ On Representing Linear Programs by Graph Neural Networks"){.ltx_ref},
we also need the following lemma whose proof is almost the same as the
proof of Lemma [[D.2]{.ltx_text
.ltx_ref_tag}](https://ar5iv.labs.arxiv.org/html/2209.12288#A4.Thmtheorem2 "Lemma D.2. ‣ Appendix D Universal approxiation of ℱ_"GNN" ‣ On Representing Linear Programs by Graph Neural Networks"){.ltx_ref}
and is hence omitted.
:::

::: {#A5.Thmtheorem3 .ltx_theorem .ltx_theorem_lemma}
###### [[Lemma E.3]{#A5.Thmtheorem3.1.1.1 .ltx_text .ltx_font_bold}]{.ltx_tag .ltx_tag_theorem}[.]{#A5.Thmtheorem3.2.2 .ltx_text .ltx_font_bold} {#lemma-e.3. .ltx_title .ltx_runin .ltx_title_theorem}

::: {#A5.Thmtheorem3.p1 .ltx_para}
$\mathcal{F}_{\text{𝐺𝑁𝑁}}^{W}$[ is a subalgebra of
$\mathcal{C}\operatorname{}{({\mathcal{G}_{m,n} \times \mathcal{H}_{m}^{V} \times \mathcal{H}_{n}^{W}},{\mathbb{R}}^{n})}$.]{#A5.Thmtheorem3.p1.2.1
.ltx_text .ltx_font_italic}
:::
:::

::: {#A5.3 .ltx_proof}
###### Proof of Theorem [[E.1]{.ltx_text .ltx_ref_tag}](https://ar5iv.labs.arxiv.org/html/2209.12288#A5.Thmtheorem1 "Theorem E.1. ‣ Appendix E Universal approximation of ℱ_"GNN"^𝑊 ‣ On Representing Linear Programs by Graph Neural Networks"){.ltx_ref}. {#proof-of-theorem-e.1. .ltx_title .ltx_runin .ltx_font_italic .ltx_title_proof}

::: {#A5.3.p1 .ltx_para}
Let $S_{m} \times S_{n}$ act on ${\mathbb{R}}^{n}$ via

  -- -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- --
     $${{{{(\sigma_{V},\sigma_{W})} \ast y} = {\sigma_{W}\operatorname{}{(y)}}},{{{\forall\sigma_{V}} \in S_{m}},{{\sigma_{W} \in S_{n}},{y \in {\mathbb{R}}^{n}}}}}.$$   
  -- -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- --

Then it follows from ([[E.1]{.ltx_text
.ltx_ref_tag}](https://ar5iv.labs.arxiv.org/html/2209.12288#A5.E1 "E.1 ‣ item (i) ‣ Theorem E.1. ‣ Appendix E Universal approximation of ℱ_"GNN"^𝑊 ‣ On Representing Linear Programs by Graph Neural Networks"){.ltx_ref})
that $\Phi$ is equivariant. In addition, the definition of graph neural
networks directly guarantees the equivariance of functions in
$\mathcal{F}_{\text{GNN}}^{W}$. Therefore, one only needs to verify the
conditions with $\mathcal{F}$ as $\mathcal{F}_{\text{GNN}}^{W}$ and
$\mathbf{G}$ as $S_{n}$ in Theorem [[E.2]{.ltx_text
.ltx_ref_tag}](https://ar5iv.labs.arxiv.org/html/2209.12288#A5.Thmtheorem2 "Theorem E.2 (Generalized Stone-Weierstrass theorem [azizian2020expressive]*Theorem 22). ‣ Appendix E Universal approximation of ℱ_"GNN"^𝑊 ‣ On Representing Linear Programs by Graph Neural Networks"){.ltx_ref}:

-   [[•]{.ltx_tag .ltx_tag_item}]{#A5.I3.i1}

    ::: {#A5.I3.i1.p1 .ltx_para}
    Condition (i) in Theorem [[E.2]{.ltx_text
    .ltx_ref_tag}](https://ar5iv.labs.arxiv.org/html/2209.12288#A5.Thmtheorem2 "Theorem E.2 (Generalized Stone-Weierstrass theorem [azizian2020expressive]*Theorem 22). ‣ Appendix E Universal approximation of ℱ_"GNN"^𝑊 ‣ On Representing Linear Programs by Graph Neural Networks"){.ltx_ref}
    follows from Lemma [[E.3]{.ltx_text
    .ltx_ref_tag}](https://ar5iv.labs.arxiv.org/html/2209.12288#A5.Thmtheorem3 "Lemma E.3. ‣ Appendix E Universal approximation of ℱ_"GNN"^𝑊 ‣ On Representing Linear Programs by Graph Neural Networks"){.ltx_ref}
    and the definition of $\mathcal{F}_{\text{GNN}}^{W}$.
    :::
-   [[•]{.ltx_tag .ltx_tag_item}]{#A5.I3.i2}

    ::: {#A5.I3.i2.p1 .ltx_para}
    Condition (ii) in Theorem [[E.2]{.ltx_text
    .ltx_ref_tag}](https://ar5iv.labs.arxiv.org/html/2209.12288#A5.Thmtheorem2 "Theorem E.2 (Generalized Stone-Weierstrass theorem [azizian2020expressive]*Theorem 22). ‣ Appendix E Universal approximation of ℱ_"GNN"^𝑊 ‣ On Representing Linear Programs by Graph Neural Networks"){.ltx_ref}
    follows from Theorem [[4.2]{.ltx_text
    .ltx_ref_tag}](https://ar5iv.labs.arxiv.org/html/2209.12288#S4.Thmtheorem2 "Theorem 4.2. ‣ Separation power ‣ 4. Sketch of proof ‣ On Representing Linear Programs by Graph Neural Networks"){.ltx_ref}
    and
    ${\mathcal{F}_{\text{GNN}}\operatorname{}\mathbf{1}} \subset \mathcal{F}_{\text{GNN}}^{W}$.
    :::
-   [[•]{.ltx_tag .ltx_tag_item}]{#A5.I3.i3}

    ::: {#A5.I3.i3.p1 .ltx_para}
    Condition (iii) in Theorem [[E.2]{.ltx_text
    .ltx_ref_tag}](https://ar5iv.labs.arxiv.org/html/2209.12288#A5.Thmtheorem2 "Theorem E.2 (Generalized Stone-Weierstrass theorem [azizian2020expressive]*Theorem 22). ‣ Appendix E Universal approximation of ℱ_"GNN"^𝑊 ‣ On Representing Linear Programs by Graph Neural Networks"){.ltx_ref}
    follows from Corollary [[C.4]{.ltx_text
    .ltx_ref_tag}](https://ar5iv.labs.arxiv.org/html/2209.12288#A3.Thmtheorem4 "Corollary C.4. ‣ Appendix C Separation power of graph neural networks ‣ On Representing Linear Programs by Graph Neural Networks"){.ltx_ref}
    and Condition (ii) in Theorem [[E.1]{.ltx_text
    .ltx_ref_tag}](https://ar5iv.labs.arxiv.org/html/2209.12288#A5.Thmtheorem1 "Theorem E.1. ‣ Appendix E Universal approximation of ℱ_"GNN"^𝑊 ‣ On Representing Linear Programs by Graph Neural Networks"){.ltx_ref}.
    :::
-   [[•]{.ltx_tag .ltx_tag_item}]{#A5.I3.i4}

    ::: {#A5.I3.i4.p1 .ltx_para}
    Condition (iv) in Theorem [[E.2]{.ltx_text
    .ltx_ref_tag}](https://ar5iv.labs.arxiv.org/html/2209.12288#A5.Thmtheorem2 "Theorem E.2 (Generalized Stone-Weierstrass theorem [azizian2020expressive]*Theorem 22). ‣ Appendix E Universal approximation of ℱ_"GNN"^𝑊 ‣ On Representing Linear Programs by Graph Neural Networks"){.ltx_ref}
    follows from Corollary [[C.5]{.ltx_text
    .ltx_ref_tag}](https://ar5iv.labs.arxiv.org/html/2209.12288#A3.Thmtheorem5 "Corollary C.5. ‣ Appendix C Separation power of graph neural networks ‣ On Representing Linear Programs by Graph Neural Networks"){.ltx_ref}
    and Condition (iii) in Theorem [[E.1]{.ltx_text
    .ltx_ref_tag}](https://ar5iv.labs.arxiv.org/html/2209.12288#A5.Thmtheorem1 "Theorem E.1. ‣ Appendix E Universal approximation of ℱ_"GNN"^𝑊 ‣ On Representing Linear Programs by Graph Neural Networks"){.ltx_ref}.
    :::

It finishes the proof. ∎
:::
:::
:::

::: {#A6 .section .ltx_appendix .ltx_indent_first}
## [Appendix F ]{.ltx_tag .ltx_tag_appendix}Proof of Main Theorems {#appendix-f-proof-of-main-theorems .ltx_title .ltx_title_appendix}

::: {#A6.p1 .ltx_para}
We collect the proofs of main theorems stated in Section [[3]{.ltx_text
.ltx_ref_tag}](https://ar5iv.labs.arxiv.org/html/2209.12288#S3 "3. Main results ‣ On Representing Linear Programs by Graph Neural Networks"){.ltx_ref}
in this section.
:::

::: {#A6.2 .ltx_proof}
###### Proof of Theorem [[3.1]{.ltx_text .ltx_ref_tag}](https://ar5iv.labs.arxiv.org/html/2209.12288#S3.Thmtheorem1 "Theorem 3.1. ‣ 3. Main results ‣ On Representing Linear Programs by Graph Neural Networks"){.ltx_ref}. {#proof-of-theorem-3.1. .ltx_title .ltx_runin .ltx_font_italic .ltx_title_proof}

::: {#A6.1.p1 .ltx_para}
If ${F\operatorname{}{(G,H)}} = {F\operatorname{}{(\hat{G},\hat{H})}}$
holds for any $F \in \mathcal{F}_{\text{GNN}}$, then
Theorem [[4.2]{.ltx_text
.ltx_ref_tag}](https://ar5iv.labs.arxiv.org/html/2209.12288#S4.Thmtheorem2 "Theorem 4.2. ‣ Separation power ‣ 4. Sketch of proof ‣ On Representing Linear Programs by Graph Neural Networks"){.ltx_ref}
guarantees that ${(G,H)} \sim {(\hat{G},\hat{H})}$. Thus, Condition (i),
(ii), and (iii) follow directly from Lemmas [[B.2]{.ltx_text
.ltx_ref_tag}](https://ar5iv.labs.arxiv.org/html/2209.12288#A2.Thmtheorem2 "Lemma B.2. ‣ Appendix B The separation power of the WL test ‣ On Representing Linear Programs by Graph Neural Networks"){.ltx_ref},
[[B.3]{.ltx_text
.ltx_ref_tag}](https://ar5iv.labs.arxiv.org/html/2209.12288#A2.Thmtheorem3 "Lemma B.3. ‣ Appendix B The separation power of the WL test ‣ On Representing Linear Programs by Graph Neural Networks"){.ltx_ref},
and [[B.4]{.ltx_text
.ltx_ref_tag}](https://ar5iv.labs.arxiv.org/html/2209.12288#A2.Thmtheorem4 "Lemma B.4. ‣ Appendix B The separation power of the WL test ‣ On Representing Linear Programs by Graph Neural Networks"){.ltx_ref},
respectively.
:::

::: {#A6.2.p2 .ltx_para}
Furthermore, if
${{F_{W}\operatorname{}{(G,H)}} = {F_{W}\operatorname{}{(\hat{G},\hat{H})}}},{{\forall F_{W}} \in \mathcal{F}_{\text{GNN}}^{W}}$,
then it follows from Corollary [[C.4]{.ltx_text
.ltx_ref_tag}](https://ar5iv.labs.arxiv.org/html/2209.12288#A3.Thmtheorem4 "Corollary C.4. ‣ Appendix C Separation power of graph neural networks ‣ On Representing Linear Programs by Graph Neural Networks"){.ltx_ref}
and Corollary [[B.7]{.ltx_text
.ltx_ref_tag}](https://ar5iv.labs.arxiv.org/html/2209.12288#A2.Thmtheorem7 "Corollary B.7. ‣ Appendix B The separation power of the WL test ‣ On Representing Linear Programs by Graph Neural Networks"){.ltx_ref}
that the two LP problems associated to $(G,H)$ and $(\hat{G},\hat{H})$
share the same optimal solution with the smallest $\ell_{2}$-norm. ∎
:::
:::

::: {#A6.p2 .ltx_para}
Then we head into the proof of three main approximation theorems, say
Theorem [[3.2]{.ltx_text
.ltx_ref_tag}](https://ar5iv.labs.arxiv.org/html/2209.12288#S3.Thmtheorem2 "Theorem 3.2. ‣ Topology and measure ‣ 3. Main results ‣ On Representing Linear Programs by Graph Neural Networks"){.ltx_ref},
[[3.4]{.ltx_text
.ltx_ref_tag}](https://ar5iv.labs.arxiv.org/html/2209.12288#S3.Thmtheorem4 "Theorem 3.4. ‣ Topology and measure ‣ 3. Main results ‣ On Representing Linear Programs by Graph Neural Networks"){.ltx_ref},
and [[3.6]{.ltx_text
.ltx_ref_tag}](https://ar5iv.labs.arxiv.org/html/2209.12288#S3.Thmtheorem6 "Theorem 3.6. ‣ Topology and measure ‣ 3. Main results ‣ On Representing Linear Programs by Graph Neural Networks"){.ltx_ref},
that state that GNNs can approximate the feasibility mapping
$\Phi_{\text{feas}}$, the optimal objective value mapping
$\Phi_{\text{obj}}$, and the optimal solution mapping
$\Phi_{\text{solu}}$ with arbitrarily small error, respectively. We have
established in Sections [[D]{.ltx_text
.ltx_ref_tag}](https://ar5iv.labs.arxiv.org/html/2209.12288#A4 "Appendix D Universal approxiation of ℱ_"GNN" ‣ On Representing Linear Programs by Graph Neural Networks"){.ltx_ref}
and [[E]{.ltx_text
.ltx_ref_tag}](https://ar5iv.labs.arxiv.org/html/2209.12288#A5 "Appendix E Universal approximation of ℱ_"GNN"^𝑊 ‣ On Representing Linear Programs by Graph Neural Networks"){.ltx_ref}
several theorems for graph neural networks to approximate continuous
mappings. Therefore, the proof of Theorem [[3.2]{.ltx_text
.ltx_ref_tag}](https://ar5iv.labs.arxiv.org/html/2209.12288#S3.Thmtheorem2 "Theorem 3.2. ‣ Topology and measure ‣ 3. Main results ‣ On Representing Linear Programs by Graph Neural Networks"){.ltx_ref},
[[3.4]{.ltx_text
.ltx_ref_tag}](https://ar5iv.labs.arxiv.org/html/2209.12288#S3.Thmtheorem4 "Theorem 3.4. ‣ Topology and measure ‣ 3. Main results ‣ On Representing Linear Programs by Graph Neural Networks"){.ltx_ref},
and [[3.6]{.ltx_text
.ltx_ref_tag}](https://ar5iv.labs.arxiv.org/html/2209.12288#S3.Thmtheorem6 "Theorem 3.6. ‣ Topology and measure ‣ 3. Main results ‣ On Representing Linear Programs by Graph Neural Networks"){.ltx_ref}
basically consists of two steps:

-   [[(i)]{.ltx_tag .ltx_tag_item}]{#A6.I1.ix1}

    ::: {#A6.I1.ix1.p1 .ltx_para}
    Show that the mappings $\Phi_{\text{feas}}$, $\Phi_{\text{obj}}$,
    and $\Phi_{\text{solu}}$ are measurable.
    :::
-   [[(ii)]{.ltx_tag .ltx_tag_item}]{#A6.I1.ix2}

    ::: {#A6.I1.ix2.p1 .ltx_para}
    Use continuous mappings to approximate the target measurable
    mappings, and then apply the universal approximation results
    established in Sections [[D]{.ltx_text
