    .ltx_ref_tag}](https://ar5iv.labs.arxiv.org/html/2209.12288#A4 "Appendix D Universal approxiation of ℱ_"GNN" ‣ On Representing Linear Programs by Graph Neural Networks"){.ltx_ref}
    and [[E]{.ltx_text
    .ltx_ref_tag}](https://ar5iv.labs.arxiv.org/html/2209.12288#A5 "Appendix E Universal approximation of ℱ_"GNN"^𝑊 ‣ On Representing Linear Programs by Graph Neural Networks"){.ltx_ref}.
    :::

Let us first prove the feasibility of $\Phi_{\text{feas}}$,
$\Phi_{\text{obj}}$, and $\Phi_{\text{solu}}$ in the following three
lemmas.
:::

::: {#A6.Thmtheorem1 .ltx_theorem .ltx_theorem_lemma}
###### [[Lemma F.1]{#A6.Thmtheorem1.1.1.1 .ltx_text .ltx_font_bold}]{.ltx_tag .ltx_tag_theorem}[.]{#A6.Thmtheorem1.2.2 .ltx_text .ltx_font_bold} {#lemma-f.1. .ltx_title .ltx_runin .ltx_title_theorem}

::: {#A6.Thmtheorem1.p1 .ltx_para}
[The feasibility mapping $\Phi_{\text{𝑓𝑒𝑎𝑠}}$ defined in
([[2.7]{.ltx_text
.ltx_ref_tag}](https://ar5iv.labs.arxiv.org/html/2209.12288#S2.E7 "2.7 ‣ Feasibility mapping ‣ 2.3. Revisiting question (P1) ‣ 2. Preliminaries ‣ On Representing Linear Programs by Graph Neural Networks"){.ltx_ref})
is measurable, i.e., the preimages
$\Phi_{\text{𝑓𝑒𝑎𝑠}}^{- 1}\operatorname{}{(0)}$ and
$\Phi_{\text{𝑓𝑒𝑎𝑠}}^{- 1}\operatorname{}{(1)}$ are both measurable
subsets of
$\mathcal{G}_{m,n} \times \mathcal{H}_{m}^{V} \times \mathcal{H}_{n}^{W}$.]{#A6.Thmtheorem1.p1.4.4
.ltx_text .ltx_font_italic}
:::
:::

::: {#A6.4 .ltx_proof}
###### Proof of Lemma [[F.1]{.ltx_text .ltx_ref_tag}](https://ar5iv.labs.arxiv.org/html/2209.12288#A6.Thmtheorem1 "Lemma F.1. ‣ Appendix F Proof of Main Theorems ‣ On Representing Linear Programs by Graph Neural Networks"){.ltx_ref}. {#proof-of-lemma-f.1. .ltx_title .ltx_runin .ltx_font_italic .ltx_title_proof}

::: {#A6.3.p1 .ltx_para}
It suffices to prove that for any $\circ \in {\{ \leq , = \geq \}}^{m}$
and any ${N_{l},N_{u}} \subset {\{ 1,2,\ldots,n\}}$, the set

  -- ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- --
     $$\begin{matrix}                                                                                                                                           
     {X_{\text{feas}}:={\{{(A,b,l,u)} \in {\mathbb{R}}^{m \times n} \times {\mathbb{R}}^{m} \times {\mathbb{R}}^{|N_{l}|} \times {\mathbb{R}}^{|N_{u}|}:}} \\   
     {\exists x \in {\mathbb{R}}^{n},\text{~s.t.~}Ax \circ b,x_{j} \geq l_{j},\forall j \in N_{l},x_{j} \leq u_{j},\forall j \in N_{u}\},}                      
     \end{matrix}$$                                                                                                                                             
  -- ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- --

is a measurable subset in
${\mathbb{R}}^{m \times n} \times {\mathbb{R}}^{m} \times {\mathbb{R}}^{|N_{l}|} \times {\mathbb{R}}^{|N_{u}|}$.
Without loss of generality, we assume that
$\circ = {( \leq ,\ldots, \leq , = ,\ldots, = , \geq ,\ldots, \geq )}$
where "$\leq$", "$=$", and "$\geq$" appear for $k_{1}$, $k_{2} - k_{1}$,
and $m - k_{1} - k_{2}$ times, respectively,
$0 \leq k_{1} \leq k_{2} \leq m$.
:::

::: {#A6.4.p2 .ltx_para}
Let us define a function
$V_{\text{feas}}:{{{\mathbb{R}}^{m \times n} \times {\mathbb{R}}^{m} \times {\mathbb{R}}^{|N_{l}|} \times {\mathbb{R}}^{|N_{u}|} \times {\mathbb{R}}^{n}}\rightarrow{\mathbb{R}}_{\geq 0}}$
that measures to what extend a point in ${\mathbb{R}}^{n}$ violates the
constraints:

  -- ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ --
     $$\begin{matrix}                                                                                                                                                                                                                                             
     {V_{\text{feas}}{(A,b,l,u,x)} = \max\left\{ \max\limits_{1 \leq i \leq k_{1}}\left( \sum\limits_{j = 1}^{n}A_{i,j}x_{j} - b_{i} \right)_{+},\max\limits_{k_{1} < i \leq k_{2}} \middle| \sum\limits_{j = 1}^{n}A_{i,j}x_{j} - b_{i} \middle| , \right.} \\   
     {\left. \max\limits_{k_{2} < i \leq m}\left( b_{i} - \sum\limits_{j = 1}^{n}A_{i,j}x_{j} \right)_{+},\max\limits_{j \in N_{l}}{(l_{j} - x_{j})}_{+},\max\limits_{j \in N_{u}}{(x_{j} - u_{j})}_{+} \right\},}                                                
     \end{matrix}$$                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               
  -- ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ --

where $y_{+} = {\max{\{ y,0\}}}$. It can be seen that $V_{\text{feas}}$
is continuous and ${V_{\text{feas}}\operatorname{}{(A,b,l,u,x)}} = 0$ if
and only if ${A\operatorname{}x} \circ b$,
${x_{j} \geq l_{j}},{{\forall j} \in N_{l}}$, and
${x_{j} \leq u_{j}},{{\forall j} \in N_{u}}$. Therefore, for any
${(A,b,l,u)} \in {{\mathbb{R}}^{m \times n} \times {\mathbb{R}}^{m} \times {\mathbb{R}}^{|N_{l}|} \times {\mathbb{R}}^{|N_{u}|}}$,
the followings are equivalent:

-   [[•]{.ltx_tag .ltx_tag_item}]{#A6.I2.i1}

    ::: {#A6.I2.i1.p1 .ltx_para}
    ${(A,b,l,u)} \in X_{\text{feas}}$.
    :::
-   [[•]{.ltx_tag .ltx_tag_item}]{#A6.I2.i2}

    ::: {#A6.I2.i2.p1 .ltx_para}
    There exists $R \in {\mathbb{N}}_{+}$ and
    $x \in B_{R}:={\{{x^{\prime} \in {\mathbb{R}}^{n}}:{{\| x^{\prime}\|} \leq R}\}}$,
    such that ${V_{\text{feas}}\operatorname{}{(A,b,l,u,x)}} = 0$.
    :::
-   [[•]{.ltx_tag .ltx_tag_item}]{#A6.I2.i3}

    ::: {#A6.I2.i3.p1 .ltx_para}
    There exists $R \in {\mathbb{N}}_{+}$ such that for any
    $r \in {\mathbb{N}}_{+}$,
    ${V_{\text{feas}}\operatorname{}{(A,b,l,u,x)}} \leq {1/r}$ holds for
    some $x \in {B_{R} \cap {\mathbb{Q}}^{n}}$.
    :::

This implies that $X_{\text{feas}}$ can be described via

  -- ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ --
     $${\bigcup\limits_{R \in {\mathbb{N}}_{+}}{\bigcap\limits_{r \in {\mathbb{N}}_{+}}{\bigcup\limits_{x \in {B_{R} \cap {\mathbb{Q}}^{n}}}\left\{ {{(A,b,l,u)} \in {{\mathbb{R}}^{m \times n} \times {\mathbb{R}}^{m} \times {\mathbb{R}}^{|N_{l}|} \times {\mathbb{R}}^{|N_{u}|}}}:{{V_{\text{feas}}\operatorname{}{(A,b,l,u,x)}} \leq \frac{1}{r}} \right\}}}}.$$   
  -- ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ --

Since ${\mathbb{Q}}^{n}$ is countable and $V$ is continuous, we
immediately obtain from the above expression that $X_{\text{feas}}$ is
measurable. ∎
:::
:::

::: {#A6.Thmtheorem2 .ltx_theorem .ltx_theorem_lemma}
###### [[Lemma F.2]{#A6.Thmtheorem2.1.1.1 .ltx_text .ltx_font_bold}]{.ltx_tag .ltx_tag_theorem}[.]{#A6.Thmtheorem2.2.2 .ltx_text .ltx_font_bold} {#lemma-f.2. .ltx_title .ltx_runin .ltx_title_theorem}

::: {#A6.Thmtheorem2.p1 .ltx_para}
[The optimal objective value mapping $\Phi_{\text{𝑜𝑏𝑗}}$ defined in
([[2.8]{.ltx_text
.ltx_ref_tag}](https://ar5iv.labs.arxiv.org/html/2209.12288#S2.E8 "2.8 ‣ Optimal objective value mapping ‣ 2.3. Revisiting question (P1) ‣ 2. Preliminaries ‣ On Representing Linear Programs by Graph Neural Networks"){.ltx_ref})
is measurable.]{#A6.Thmtheorem2.p1.1.1 .ltx_text .ltx_font_italic}
:::
:::

::: {#A6.5 .ltx_proof}
###### Proof of Lemma [[F.2]{.ltx_text .ltx_ref_tag}](https://ar5iv.labs.arxiv.org/html/2209.12288#A6.Thmtheorem2 "Lemma F.2. ‣ Appendix F Proof of Main Theorems ‣ On Representing Linear Programs by Graph Neural Networks"){.ltx_ref}. {#proof-of-lemma-f.2. .ltx_title .ltx_runin .ltx_font_italic .ltx_title_proof}

::: {#A6.5.p1 .ltx_para}
It suffices to prove that for any $\circ \in {\{ \leq , = \geq \}}^{m}$,
any subsets ${N_{l},N_{u}} \subset {\{ 1,2,\ldots,n\}}$, and any
$\phi \in {\mathbb{R}}$, the set

  -- ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- --
     $$\begin{matrix}                                                                                                                                                                    
     {X_{\text{obj}}:={\{{(A,b,c,l,u)} \in {\mathbb{R}}^{m \times n} \times {\mathbb{R}}^{m} \times {\mathbb{R}}^{n} \times {\mathbb{R}}^{|N_{l}|} \times {\mathbb{R}}^{|N_{u}|}:}} \\   
     {\exists x \in {\mathbb{R}}^{n},\text{~s.t.~}c^{\top}x \leq \phi,Ax \circ b,x_{j} \geq l_{j},\forall j \in N_{l},x_{j} \leq u_{j},\forall j \in N_{u}\},}                           
     \end{matrix}$$                                                                                                                                                                      
  -- ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- --

is a measurable subset in
${\mathbb{R}}^{m \times n} \times {\mathbb{R}}^{m} \times {\mathbb{R}}^{n} \times {\mathbb{R}}^{|N_{l}|} \times {\mathbb{R}}^{|N_{u}|}$.
Then the proof follows the same lines as in the proof of
Lemma [[F.1]{.ltx_text
.ltx_ref_tag}](https://ar5iv.labs.arxiv.org/html/2209.12288#A6.Thmtheorem1 "Lemma F.1. ‣ Appendix F Proof of Main Theorems ‣ On Representing Linear Programs by Graph Neural Networks"){.ltx_ref},
with a different violation function

  -- -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- --
     $${{V_{\text{obj}}\operatorname{}{(A,b,c,l,u,x)}} = {\max\left\{ {({{c^{\top}\operatorname{}x} - \phi})}_{+},{V_{\text{feas}}\operatorname{}{(A,b,l,u,x)}} \right\}}}.$$   
  -- -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- --

∎
:::
:::

::: {#A6.Thmtheorem3 .ltx_theorem .ltx_theorem_lemma}
###### [[Lemma F.3]{#A6.Thmtheorem3.1.1.1 .ltx_text .ltx_font_bold}]{.ltx_tag .ltx_tag_theorem}[.]{#A6.Thmtheorem3.2.2 .ltx_text .ltx_font_bold} {#lemma-f.3. .ltx_title .ltx_runin .ltx_title_theorem}

::: {#A6.Thmtheorem3.p1 .ltx_para}
[The optimal solution mapping $\Phi_{\text{𝑠𝑜𝑙𝑢}}$ defined in
([[2.9]{.ltx_text
.ltx_ref_tag}](https://ar5iv.labs.arxiv.org/html/2209.12288#S2.E9 "2.9 ‣ Optimal solution mapping ‣ 2.3. Revisiting question (P1) ‣ 2. Preliminaries ‣ On Representing Linear Programs by Graph Neural Networks"){.ltx_ref})
is measurable.]{#A6.Thmtheorem3.p1.1.1 .ltx_text .ltx_font_italic}
:::
:::

::: {#A6.7 .ltx_proof}
###### Proof of Lemma [[F.3]{.ltx_text .ltx_ref_tag}](https://ar5iv.labs.arxiv.org/html/2209.12288#A6.Thmtheorem3 "Lemma F.3. ‣ Appendix F Proof of Main Theorems ‣ On Representing Linear Programs by Graph Neural Networks"){.ltx_ref}. {#proof-of-lemma-f.3. .ltx_title .ltx_runin .ltx_font_italic .ltx_title_proof}

::: {#A6.6.p1 .ltx_para}
It suffices to show that for every $j_{0} \in {\{ 1,2,\ldots,n\}}$, the
mapping

  -- --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- --
     $${{\pi_{j_{0}} \circ \Phi_{\text{solu}}}:{{\Phi_{\text{obj}}^{- 1}\operatorname{}{({\mathbb{R}})}}\rightarrow{\mathbb{R}}}},$$   
  -- --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- --

is measurable, where
$\pi_{j_{0}}:{{\mathbb{R}}^{n}\rightarrow{\mathbb{R}}}$ maps a vector
$x \in {\mathbb{R}}^{n}$ to its $j_{0}$-th component. Similar as before,
one can consider any $\circ \in {\{ \leq , = \geq \}}^{m}$, any
${N_{l},N_{u}} \subset {\{ 1,2,\ldots,n\}}$, and any
$\phi \in {\mathbb{R}}$, and prove that the set

  -- ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ --
     $$\begin{matrix}                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                         
     {X_{\text{solu}}:={\{{(A,b,c,l,u)} \in {\mathbb{R}}^{m \times n} \times {\mathbb{R}}^{m} \times {\mathbb{R}}^{n} \times {\mathbb{R}}^{|N_{l}|} \times {\mathbb{R}}^{|N_{u}|}:}} \\                                                                                       
     {{{{{\text{The LP problem,~}\operatorname{}{\min\limits_{x \in {\mathbb{R}}^{d}}{c^{\top}\operatorname{}x}}},{{\text{~s.t.~}\operatorname{}A\operatorname{}x} \circ b},x_{j}} \geq l_{j}},{{{\forall j} \in N_{l}},{{x_{j} \leq u_{j}},{{\forall j} \in N_{u}}}}},} \\   
     {{{\text{has a finite optimal objective value, and its optimal solution with the smallest~}\operatorname{}\ell_{2}} - \text{norm}},} \\                                                                                                                                  
     {x_{\text{opt}},\text{~satisfies~}{(x_{\text{opt}})}_{j_{0}} < \phi\},}                                                                                                                                                                                                  
     \end{matrix}$$                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                           
  -- ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ --

is measurable.
:::

::: {#A6.7.p2 .ltx_para}
Note that we have fixed $\circ \in {\{ \leq , = \geq \}}^{m}$ and
${N_{l},N_{u}} \subset {\{ 1,2,\ldots,n\}}$. Let

  -- -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- --
     $${\iota:{{{\mathbb{R}}^{m \times n} \times {\mathbb{R}}^{m} \times {\mathbb{R}}^{n} \times {\mathbb{R}}^{|N_{l}|} \times {\mathbb{R}}^{|N_{u}|}}\rightarrow{\mathcal{G}_{m,n} \times \mathcal{H}_{m}^{V} \times \mathcal{H}_{n}^{W}}}},$$   
  -- -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- --

be the embedding map. Define another violation function

  -- ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ --
     $${V_{\text{solu}}:{{{{({\Phi_{\text{obj}} \circ \iota})}^{- 1}\operatorname{}{({\mathbb{R}})}} \times {\mathbb{R}}^{n}}\rightarrow{\mathbb{R}}}},$$   
  -- ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ --

via

  -- ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- --
     $${{V_{\text{solu}}\operatorname{}{(A,b,c,l,u,x)}} = {\max\left\{ \left( {{c^{\top}\operatorname{}x} - {\Phi_{\text{obj}}\operatorname{}{({\iota\operatorname{}{(A,b,c,l,u)}})}}} \right)_{+},{V_{\text{feas}}\operatorname{}{(A,b,c,l,u,x)}} \right\}}},$$   
  -- ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- --

which is measurable due to the measurability of $\Phi_{\text{obj}}$ and
the continuity of $V_{\text{feas}}$. Moreover, $V_{\text{solu}}$ is
continuous with respect to $x$. Therefore, the followings are equivalent
for
${(A,b,c,l,u)} \in {{({\Phi_{\text{obj}} \circ \iota})}^{- 1}\operatorname{}{({\mathbb{R}})}}$:

-   [[•]{.ltx_tag .ltx_tag_item}]{#A6.I3.i1}

    ::: {#A6.I3.i1.p1 .ltx_para}
    ${(A,b,c,l,u)} \in X_{\text{solu}}$.
    :::
-   [[•]{.ltx_tag .ltx_tag_item}]{#A6.I3.i2}

    ::: {#A6.I3.i2.p1 .ltx_para}
    There exists $x \in {\mathbb{R}}^{n}$ with $x_{j_{0}} < \phi$, such
    that ${V_{\text{solu}}\operatorname{}{(A,b,c,l,u,x)}} = 0$ and
    ${{V_{\text{solu}}\operatorname{}{(A,b,c,l,u,x^{\prime})}} > 0},{{{\forall x^{\prime}} \in B_{\| x\|}},{x_{j_{0}}^{\prime} \geq \phi}}$.
    :::
-   [[•]{.ltx_tag .ltx_tag_item}]{#A6.I3.i3}

    ::: {#A6.I3.i3.p1 .ltx_para}
    There exists $R \in {\mathbb{Q}}_{+}$, $r \in {\mathbb{N}}_{+}$, and
    $x \in B_{R}$ with $x_{j_{0}} \leq {\phi - {1/r}}$, such that
    ${V_{\text{solu}}\operatorname{}{(A,b,c,l,u,x)}} = 0$ and
    ${{V_{\text{solu}}\operatorname{}{(A,b,c,l,u,x^{\prime})}} > 0},{{{\forall x^{\prime}} \in B_{R}},{x_{j_{0}}^{\prime} \geq \phi}}$.
    :::
-   [[•]{.ltx_tag .ltx_tag_item}]{#A6.I3.i4}

    ::: {#A6.I3.i4.p1 .ltx_para}
    There exists $R \in {\mathbb{Q}}_{+}$ and $r \in {\mathbb{N}}_{+}$,
    such that for all $r^{\prime} \in {\mathbb{N}}_{+}$,
    ${\exists x} \in {B_{R} \cap {\mathbb{Q}}^{n}}$,
    $x_{j_{0}} \leq {\phi - {1/r}}$, s.t.
    ${V_{\text{solu}}\operatorname{}{(A,b,c,l,u,x)}} < {1/r^{\prime}}$
    and that
    ${\exists r^{\operatorname{\prime\prime}}} \in {\mathbb{N}}_{+}$,
    s.t.,
    ${{V_{\text{solu}}\operatorname{}{(A,b,c,l,u,x^{\prime})}} \geq {1/r^{\operatorname{\prime\prime}}}},{{{\forall x^{\prime}} \in {B_{R} \cap {\mathbb{Q}}^{n}}},{x_{j_{0}}^{\prime} \geq \phi}}$.
    :::

Therefore, one has that

  -- -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- --
     $$\begin{matrix}                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                             
     {X_{\text{solu}} = {\bigcup\limits_{R \in {\mathbb{Q}}_{+}}\bigcup\limits_{r \in {\mathbb{N}}_{+}}}} \\                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      
     \left( \left( \bigcap\limits_{r^{\prime} \in {\mathbb{N}}_{+}}\bigcup\limits_{{x \in {B_{R} \cap {\mathbb{Q}}^{n}}},{x_{j_{0}} \leq {\phi - \frac{1}{r}}}}\left\{ {(A,b,c,l,u)} \in {(\Phi_{\text{obj}} \circ \iota)}^{- 1}{({\mathbb{R}})}:V_{\text{solu}}{(A,b,c,l,u,x)} < \frac{1}{r^{\prime}} \right\} \right) \right. \\                                                                
     {\left. \cap \left( \bigcup\limits_{r^{\operatorname{\prime\prime}} \in {\mathbb{N}}_{+}}\bigcap\limits_{{x^{\prime} \in {B_{R} \cap {\mathbb{Q}}^{n}}},{x_{j_{0}}^{\prime} \geq \phi}}\left\{ {(A,b,c,l,u)} \in {(\Phi_{\text{obj}} \circ \iota)}^{- 1}{({\mathbb{R}})}:V_{\text{solu}}{(A,b,c,l,u,x^{\prime})} \geq \frac{1}{r^{\operatorname{\prime\prime}}} \right\} \right) \right),}   
     \end{matrix}$$                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               
  -- -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- --

which is measurable. ∎
:::
:::

::: {#A6.p3 .ltx_para}
With the measurability of $\Phi_{\text{feas}}$, $\Phi_{\text{obj}}$, and
$\Phi_{\text{solu}}$ established, the next step is to approximate
$\Phi_{\text{feas}}$, $\Phi_{\text{obj}}$, and $\Phi_{\text{solu}}$
using continuous mappings, and hence graph neural networks. Before
proceeding, let us mention that
$\mathcal{G}_{m,n} \times \mathcal{H}_{m}^{V} \times \mathcal{H}_{n}^{W}$
is essentially the disjoint union of finitely many product spaces of
Euclidean spaces and discrete spaces that have finitely many points and
are equipped with discrete measures. More specifically,

  -- --------------------------------------------------------------------------- --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- --
     $\mathcal{G}_{m,n} \times \mathcal{H}_{m}^{V} \times \mathcal{H}_{n}^{W}$   $\cong {{\mathbb{R}}^{m \times n} \times {({{\mathbb{R}} \times {\{ \leq , = , \geq \}}})}^{m} \times {({{\mathbb{R}} \times {({{\mathbb{R}} \cup {\{{- \infty}\}}})} \times {({{\mathbb{R}} \cup {\{{+ \infty}\}}})}})}^{n}}$                                                                      
                                                                                 $\cong {\bigcup\limits_{{k,k^{\prime}} = 0}^{n}{\bigcup\limits_{s = 1}^{(\binom{n}{k})}{\bigcup\limits_{s^{\prime} = 1}^{(\binom{n}{k^{\prime}})}{{\mathbb{R}}^{m \times n} \times {\mathbb{R}}^{m} \times {\mathbb{R}}^{n} \times {\mathbb{R}}^{n - k} \times {\mathbb{R}}^{n - k^{\prime}}}}}}$   
                                                                                 ${\times {{\{ \leq , = , \geq \}}^{m} \times {\{{- \infty}\}}^{k} \times {\{{+ \infty}\}}^{k^{\prime}}}}.$                                                                                                                                                                                          
  -- --------------------------------------------------------------------------- --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- --

Therefore, many results in real analysis for Euclidean spaces still
apply for
$\mathcal{G}_{m,n} \times \mathcal{H}_{m}^{V} \times \mathcal{H}_{n}^{W}$
and $\text{Meas}\operatorname{}{( \cdot )}$, including the following
Lusin's theorem.
:::

::: {#A6.Thmtheorem4 .ltx_theorem .ltx_theorem_theorem}
###### [[Theorem F.4]{#A6.Thmtheorem4.1.1.1 .ltx_text .ltx_font_bold}]{.ltx_tag .ltx_tag_theorem}[ ]{#A6.Thmtheorem4.2.2 .ltx_text .ltx_font_bold}(Lusin's theorem \[[evans2018measure]{.ltx_ref .ltx_missing_citation .ltx_ref_self}\]\*Theorem 1.14)[.]{#A6.Thmtheorem4.3.3 .ltx_text .ltx_font_bold} {#theorem-f.4-lusins-theorem-evans2018measuretheorem-1.14. .ltx_title .ltx_runin .ltx_title_theorem}

::: {#A6.Thmtheorem4.p1 .ltx_para}
[Let $\mu$ be a Borel regular measure on ${\mathbb{R}}^{n}$ and let
$f:{{\mathbb{R}}^{n}\rightarrow{\mathbb{R}}^{m}}$ be $\mu$-measurable.
Then for any $\mu$-measurable $X \subset {\mathbb{R}}^{n}$ with
${\mu\operatorname{}{(X)}} < \infty$ and any $\epsilon > 0$, there
exists a compact set $E \subset X$ with
${\mu\operatorname{}{({X\backslash E})}} < \epsilon$, such that
${f|}_{E}$ is continuous.]{#A6.Thmtheorem4.p1.11.11 .ltx_text
.ltx_font_italic}
:::
:::

::: {#A6.8 .ltx_proof}
###### Proof of Theorem [[3.2]{.ltx_text .ltx_ref_tag}](https://ar5iv.labs.arxiv.org/html/2209.12288#S3.Thmtheorem2 "Theorem 3.2. ‣ Topology and measure ‣ 3. Main results ‣ On Representing Linear Programs by Graph Neural Networks"){.ltx_ref}. {#proof-of-theorem-3.2. .ltx_title .ltx_runin .ltx_font_italic .ltx_title_proof}

::: {#A6.8.p1 .ltx_para}
Since
$X \subset {\mathcal{G}_{m,n} \times \mathcal{H}_{m}^{V} \times \mathcal{H}_{n}^{W}}$
is measurable with finite measure, according to Lusin's theorem, there
is a compact set $E \subset X$ such that ${\Phi_{\text{feas}}|}_{E}$ is
continuous with
${\text{Meas}\operatorname{}{({X\backslash E})}} < \epsilon$. By
Lemma [[B.2]{.ltx_text
.ltx_ref_tag}](https://ar5iv.labs.arxiv.org/html/2209.12288#A2.Thmtheorem2 "Lemma B.2. ‣ Appendix B The separation power of the WL test ‣ On Representing Linear Programs by Graph Neural Networks"){.ltx_ref}
and Theorem [[4.3]{.ltx_text
.ltx_ref_tag}](https://ar5iv.labs.arxiv.org/html/2209.12288#S4.Thmtheorem3 "Theorem 4.3. ‣ Representation power ‣ 4. Sketch of proof ‣ On Representing Linear Programs by Graph Neural Networks"){.ltx_ref},
there exists $F \in \mathcal{F}_{\text{GNN}}$ such that

  -- -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- --
     $${{\sup\limits_{{(G,H)} \in E}{|{{F\operatorname{}{(G,H)}} - {\Phi_{\text{feas}}\operatorname{}{(G,H)}}}|}} < \frac{1}{2}},$$   
  -- -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- --

which implies that

  -- --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- --
     $${{{\mathbb{I}}_{{F\operatorname{}{(G,H)}} > {1/2}} = {\Phi_{\text{feas}}\operatorname{}{(G,H)}}},{{\forall{(G,H)}} \in E}}.$$   
  -- --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- --

Therefore, one obtains

  -- ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- --
     $${{\text{Meas}\operatorname{}\left( \left\{ {{(G,H)} \in X}:{{\mathbb{I}}_{{F\operatorname{}{(G,H)}} > {1/2}} \neq {\Phi_{\text{feas}}\operatorname{}{(G,H)}}} \right\} \right)} \leq {\text{Meas}\operatorname{}{({X\backslash E})}} < \epsilon},$$   
  -- ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- --

and the proof is completed. ∎
:::
:::

::: {#A6.9 .ltx_proof}
###### Proof of Corollary [[3.3]{.ltx_text .ltx_ref_tag}](https://ar5iv.labs.arxiv.org/html/2209.12288#S3.Thmtheorem3 "Corollary 3.3. ‣ Topology and measure ‣ 3. Main results ‣ On Representing Linear Programs by Graph Neural Networks"){.ltx_ref}. {#proof-of-corollary-3.3. .ltx_title .ltx_runin .ltx_font_italic .ltx_title_proof}

::: {#A6.9.p1 .ltx_para}
As a finite set, $\mathcal{D}$ is compact and
${\Phi_{\text{feas}}|}_{\mathcal{D}}$ is continuous. The rest of the
proof is similar to that of Theorem [[3.2]{.ltx_text
.ltx_ref_tag}](https://ar5iv.labs.arxiv.org/html/2209.12288#S3.Thmtheorem2 "Theorem 3.2. ‣ Topology and measure ‣ 3. Main results ‣ On Representing Linear Programs by Graph Neural Networks"){.ltx_ref},
using Lemma [[B.2]{.ltx_text
.ltx_ref_tag}](https://ar5iv.labs.arxiv.org/html/2209.12288#A2.Thmtheorem2 "Lemma B.2. ‣ Appendix B The separation power of the WL test ‣ On Representing Linear Programs by Graph Neural Networks"){.ltx_ref}
and Theorem [[4.3]{.ltx_text
.ltx_ref_tag}](https://ar5iv.labs.arxiv.org/html/2209.12288#S4.Thmtheorem3 "Theorem 4.3. ‣ Representation power ‣ 4. Sketch of proof ‣ On Representing Linear Programs by Graph Neural Networks"){.ltx_ref}.
∎
:::
:::

::: {#A6.11 .ltx_proof}
###### Proof of Theorem [[3.4]{.ltx_text .ltx_ref_tag}](https://ar5iv.labs.arxiv.org/html/2209.12288#S3.Thmtheorem4 "Theorem 3.4. ‣ Topology and measure ‣ 3. Main results ‣ On Representing Linear Programs by Graph Neural Networks"){.ltx_ref}. {#proof-of-theorem-3.4. .ltx_title .ltx_runin .ltx_font_italic .ltx_title_proof}

::: {#A6.10.p1 .ltx_para}
\(i\) The proof follows the same lines as in the proof of
Theorem [[3.2]{.ltx_text
.ltx_ref_tag}](https://ar5iv.labs.arxiv.org/html/2209.12288#S3.Thmtheorem2 "Theorem 3.2. ‣ Topology and measure ‣ 3. Main results ‣ On Representing Linear Programs by Graph Neural Networks"){.ltx_ref},
with the difference that we approximate

  -- ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- --
     $${\Phi\operatorname{}{(G,H)}} = \begin{cases}                                                       
     {1,} & {{{\text{if~}\operatorname{}\Phi_{\text{obj}}\operatorname{}{(G,H)}} \in {\mathbb{R}}},} \\   
     {0,} & {\text{otherwise},}                                                                           
     \end{cases}$$                                                                                        
  -- ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- --

instead of $\Phi_{\text{feas}}$ and we use Lemma [[B.3]{.ltx_text
.ltx_ref_tag}](https://ar5iv.labs.arxiv.org/html/2209.12288#A2.Thmtheorem3 "Lemma B.3. ‣ Appendix B The separation power of the WL test ‣ On Representing Linear Programs by Graph Neural Networks"){.ltx_ref}
instead of Lemma [[B.2]{.ltx_text
.ltx_ref_tag}](https://ar5iv.labs.arxiv.org/html/2209.12288#A2.Thmtheorem2 "Lemma B.2. ‣ Appendix B The separation power of the WL test ‣ On Representing Linear Programs by Graph Neural Networks"){.ltx_ref}.
:::

::: {#A6.11.p2 .ltx_para}
\(ii\) The proof is still similar to that of Theorem [[3.2]{.ltx_text
.ltx_ref_tag}](https://ar5iv.labs.arxiv.org/html/2209.12288#S3.Thmtheorem2 "Theorem 3.2. ‣ Topology and measure ‣ 3. Main results ‣ On Representing Linear Programs by Graph Neural Networks"){.ltx_ref}.
By Lusin's theorem, there is a compact set
$E \subset {X \cap {\Phi_{\text{obj}}^{- 1}\operatorname{}{({\mathbb{R}})}}}$
such that ${\Phi_{\text{obj}}|}_{E}$ is continuous with
${\text{Meas}\operatorname{}\left( {{({X \cap {\Phi_{\text{obj}}^{- 1}\operatorname{}{({\mathbb{R}})}}})}\backslash E} \right)} < \epsilon$.
Using Lemma [[B.3]{.ltx_text
.ltx_ref_tag}](https://ar5iv.labs.arxiv.org/html/2209.12288#A2.Thmtheorem3 "Lemma B.3. ‣ Appendix B The separation power of the WL test ‣ On Representing Linear Programs by Graph Neural Networks"){.ltx_ref}
and Theorem [[4.3]{.ltx_text
.ltx_ref_tag}](https://ar5iv.labs.arxiv.org/html/2209.12288#S4.Thmtheorem3 "Theorem 4.3. ‣ Representation power ‣ 4. Sketch of proof ‣ On Representing Linear Programs by Graph Neural Networks"){.ltx_ref},
there exists $F_{2} \in \mathcal{F}_{\text{GNN}}$ with

  -- ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ --
     $${{\sup\limits_{{(G,H)} \in E}{|{{F_{2}\operatorname{}{(G,H)}} - {\Phi_{\text{obj}}\operatorname{}{(G,H)}}}|}} < \delta},$$   
  -- ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ --

which implies that

  -- ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ --
     $${{\text{Meas}\operatorname{}\left( \left\{ {{(G,H)} \in X}:{{|{{F_{2}\operatorname{}{(G,H)}} - {\Phi_{\text{obj}}\operatorname{}{(G,H)}}}|} > \delta} \right\} \right)} \leq {\text{Meas}\operatorname{}\left( {{({X \cap {\Phi_{\text{obj}}^{- 1}\operatorname{}{({\mathbb{R}})}}})}\backslash E} \right)} < \epsilon}.$$   
  -- ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ --

∎
:::
:::

::: {#A6.12 .ltx_proof}
###### Proof of Corollary [[3.5]{.ltx_text .ltx_ref_tag}](https://ar5iv.labs.arxiv.org/html/2209.12288#S3.Thmtheorem5 "Corollary 3.5. ‣ Topology and measure ‣ 3. Main results ‣ On Representing Linear Programs by Graph Neural Networks"){.ltx_ref}. {#proof-of-corollary-3.5. .ltx_title .ltx_runin .ltx_font_italic .ltx_title_proof}

::: {#A6.12.p1 .ltx_para}
The results can be proved using similar techniques as in
Theorem [[3.2]{.ltx_text
.ltx_ref_tag}](https://ar5iv.labs.arxiv.org/html/2209.12288#S3.Thmtheorem2 "Theorem 3.2. ‣ Topology and measure ‣ 3. Main results ‣ On Representing Linear Programs by Graph Neural Networks"){.ltx_ref}
and Theorem [[3.4]{.ltx_text
.ltx_ref_tag}](https://ar5iv.labs.arxiv.org/html/2209.12288#S3.Thmtheorem4 "Theorem 3.4. ‣ Topology and measure ‣ 3. Main results ‣ On Representing Linear Programs by Graph Neural Networks"){.ltx_ref}
by noticing that any finite dataset is compact on which any real-valued
function is continuous. ∎
:::
:::

::: {#A6.13 .ltx_proof}
###### Proof of Theorem [[3.6]{.ltx_text .ltx_ref_tag}](https://ar5iv.labs.arxiv.org/html/2209.12288#S3.Thmtheorem6 "Theorem 3.6. ‣ Topology and measure ‣ 3. Main results ‣ On Representing Linear Programs by Graph Neural Networks"){.ltx_ref}. {#proof-of-theorem-3.6. .ltx_title .ltx_runin .ltx_font_italic .ltx_title_proof}

::: {#A6.13.p1 .ltx_para}
By Lusin's theorem, there exists a compact subset $E^{\prime} \subset X$
such that
${\text{Meas}\operatorname{}{({A\backslash X})}} < {\epsilon/{|{S_{m} \times S_{n}}|}}$
and that ${\Phi_{\text{solu}}|}_{E^{\prime}}$ is continuous. Define
another compact set:

  -- --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- --
     $${E = {\bigcap\limits_{{(\sigma_{V},\sigma_{W})} \in {S_{m} \times S_{n}}}{{(\sigma_{V},\sigma_{W})} \ast E^{\prime}}} \subset X},$$   
  -- --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- --

which is closed under the action of $S_{m} \times S_{n}$ and satisfies

  -- ------------------------------------------------- ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- --
     $\text{Meas}\operatorname{}{({X\backslash E})}$   $\leq {\sum\limits_{{(\sigma_{V},\sigma_{W})} \in {S_{m} \times S_{n}}}{\text{Meas}\operatorname{}{({{X\backslash{(\sigma_{V},\sigma_{W})}} \ast E^{\prime}})}}}$                                 
                                                       $= {\sum\limits_{{(\sigma_{V},\sigma_{W})} \in {S_{m} \times S_{n}}}{\text{Meas}\operatorname{}{({{{{(\sigma_{V},\sigma_{W})} \ast X}\backslash{(\sigma_{V},\sigma_{W})}} \ast E^{\prime}})}}}$   
                                                       $= {\sum\limits_{{(\sigma_{V},\sigma_{W})} \in {S_{m} \times S_{n}}}{\mu\operatorname{}{({{(\sigma_{V},\sigma_{W})} \ast {({X\backslash E^{\prime}})}})}}}$                                       
                                                       $= {\sum\limits_{{(\sigma_{V},\sigma_{W})} \in {S_{m} \times S_{n}}}{\mu\operatorname{}{({X\backslash E^{\prime}})}}}$                                                                            
                                                       $< {\sum\limits_{{(\sigma_{V},\sigma_{W})} \in {S_{m} \times S_{n}}}\frac{\epsilon}{|{S_{m} \times S_{n}}|}}$                                                                                     
                                                       ${= \epsilon}.$                                                                                                                                                                                   
  -- ------------------------------------------------- ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- --

Note that the three conditions in Theorem [[E.1]{.ltx_text
.ltx_ref_tag}](https://ar5iv.labs.arxiv.org/html/2209.12288#A5.Thmtheorem1 "Theorem E.1. ‣ Appendix E Universal approximation of ℱ_"GNN"^𝑊 ‣ On Representing Linear Programs by Graph Neural Networks"){.ltx_ref}
are satisfied by the definition of $\Phi_{\text{solu}}$,
Corollary [[B.7]{.ltx_text
.ltx_ref_tag}](https://ar5iv.labs.arxiv.org/html/2209.12288#A2.Thmtheorem7 "Corollary B.7. ‣ Appendix B The separation power of the WL test ‣ On Representing Linear Programs by Graph Neural Networks"){.ltx_ref},
and Corollary [[B.6]{.ltx_text
.ltx_ref_tag}](https://ar5iv.labs.arxiv.org/html/2209.12288#A2.Thmtheorem6 "Definition B.6. ‣ Appendix B The separation power of the WL test ‣ On Representing Linear Programs by Graph Neural Networks"){.ltx_ref},
respectively. Using Theorem [[E.1]{.ltx_text
.ltx_ref_tag}](https://ar5iv.labs.arxiv.org/html/2209.12288#A5.Thmtheorem1 "Theorem E.1. ‣ Appendix E Universal approximation of ℱ_"GNN"^𝑊 ‣ On Representing Linear Programs by Graph Neural Networks"){.ltx_ref},
there exists $F_{W} \in \mathcal{F}_{\text{GNN}}^{W}$ such that

  -- --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- --
     $${{\sup\limits_{{(G,H)} \in E}{\|{{F_{W}\operatorname{}{(G,H)}} - {\Phi_{\text{solu}}\operatorname{}{(G,H)}}}\|}} < \delta}.$$   
  -- --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- --

Therefore, it holds that

  -- -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- --
     $${{\text{Meas}\operatorname{}\left( \left\{ {{(G,H)} \in X}:{{\|{{F_{W}\operatorname{}{(G,H)}} - {\Phi_{\text{solu}}\operatorname{}{(G,H)}}}\|} > \delta} \right\} \right)} \leq {\text{Meas}\operatorname{}{({X\backslash E})}} < \epsilon},$$   
  -- -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- --

which completes the proof. ∎
:::
:::

::: {#A6.14 .ltx_proof}
###### Proof of Corollary [[3.7]{.ltx_text .ltx_ref_tag}](https://ar5iv.labs.arxiv.org/html/2209.12288#S3.Thmtheorem7 "Corollary 3.7. ‣ Topology and measure ‣ 3. Main results ‣ On Representing Linear Programs by Graph Neural Networks"){.ltx_ref}. {#proof-of-corollary-3.7. .ltx_title .ltx_runin .ltx_font_italic .ltx_title_proof}

::: {#A6.14.p1 .ltx_para}
One can assume that $\mathcal{D}$ is closed under the action of
$S_{m} \times S_{n}$; otherwise, a larger but still finite dataset,
$\bigcup_{{(\sigma_{V},\sigma_{W})} \in {S_{m} \times S_{n}}}{{(\sigma_{V},\sigma_{W})} \ast \mathcal{D}}$,
can be considered instead of $\mathcal{D}$. The rest of the proof is
similar to that of Theorem [[3.6]{.ltx_text
.ltx_ref_tag}](https://ar5iv.labs.arxiv.org/html/2209.12288#S3.Thmtheorem6 "Theorem 3.6. ‣ Topology and measure ‣ 3. Main results ‣ On Representing Linear Programs by Graph Neural Networks"){.ltx_ref}
since $\mathcal{D}$ is compact and ${\Phi_{\text{solu}}|}_{\mathcal{D}}$
is continuous. ∎
:::
:::
:::

::: {#A7 .section .ltx_appendix .ltx_indent_first}
## [Appendix G ]{.ltx_tag .ltx_tag_appendix}Details of the numerical experiments {#appendix-g-details-of-the-numerical-experiments .ltx_title .ltx_title_appendix}

::: {#A7.SS0.SSS0.Px1 .section .ltx_paragraph .ltx_indentfirst}
#### LP instance generation {#lp-instance-generation .ltx_title .ltx_font_bold .ltx_title_paragraph}

::: {#A7.SS0.SSS0.Px1.p1 .ltx_para}
We generate each LP with the following way. We set $m = 10$ and
$n = 50$. Each matrix $A$ is sparse with $100$ nonzero elements whose
positions are sampled uniformly and values are sampled normally. Each
element in $b,c$ are sampled i.i.d and uniformly from
$\lbrack{- 1},1\rbrack$. The variable bounds $l,u$ are sampled with
$\mathcal{N}\operatorname{}{(0,10)}$. If $l_{j} > u_{j}$, then we swap
$l_{j}$ and $u_{j}$ for all $1 \leq j \leq n$. Furthermore, we sample
$\circ_{i}$ i.i.d with ${\mathbb{P}}{( \circ_{i} = `` \leq ")} = 0.7$
and ${\mathbb{P}}{( \circ_{i} = `` = ")} = 0.3$. With the generation
approach above, the probability that each LP to be feasible is around
$0.53$.
:::
:::

::: {#A7.SS0.SSS0.Px2 .section .ltx_paragraph .ltx_indentfirst}
#### MLP architectures {#mlp-architectures .ltx_title .ltx_font_bold .ltx_title_paragraph}

::: {#A7.SS0.SSS0.Px2.p1 .ltx_para}
As we mentions in the main text, all the learnable functions in GNN are
taken as MLPs. The input functions $f_{in}^{V},f_{in}^{W}$ have one
hidden layer and other functions
$f_{\text{out}},f_{\text{out}}^{W},{\{ f_{l}^{V},f_{l}^{W},g_{l}^{V},g_{l}^{W}\}}_{l = 0}^{L}$
have two hidden layers. The embedding size $d_{0},\cdots,d_{L}$ are
uniformly taken as $d$ that is chosen from
$\{ 2,4,8,16,32,64,128,256,512\}$. All the activation functions are
ReLU.
:::
:::

::: {#A7.SS0.SSS0.Px3 .section .ltx_paragraph .ltx_indentfirst}
#### Training settings {#training-settings .ltx_title .ltx_font_bold .ltx_title_paragraph}

::: {#A7.SS0.SSS0.Px3.p1 .ltx_para}
We use Adam \[[kingma2014adam]{.ltx_ref .ltx_missing_citation
.ltx_ref_self}\] as our training optimizer with learning rate of
$0.0003$. The loss function is taken as mean squared error. All the
experiments are conducted on a Linux server with an Intel Xeon Platinum
8163 GPU and eight NVIDIA Tesla V100 GPUs.
:::
